maps.area: Scripted Intro Flow (pre-LLM session opening) — TestChat.tsx orchestration
maps.summary:
The scripted intro is a deterministic client-side state machine in apps/studio/src/panels/TestChat.tsx, keyed on `scriptedSessionStepRef.current` (a `ScriptedSessionStep` string, lines 55-68). It runs entirely on the client BEFORE the LLM ever takes over: every step speaks a fixed (config-overridable) line, classifies the child's reply via the Node brain's /api/classify endpoint, and transitions. Constants are file-level (lines 172-369) but each is overridden by `conversationFlow.*` from /api/config (getters at lines 1049-1072; default.json lines 614-648). The intro never calls /api/chat — only /api/classify, /api/risk-assess (safety, every turn), and /api/tts/visemes (every spoken line). Age is captured by `parseAgeFromText` (lines 325-352, handles ASCII + Chinese numerals 一-九/两/十/百), validated to [1,120], stored in `childAgeRef.current` + `setChildAge`, and becomes the source of truth for all later LLM/system-prompt/Voice-Live calls (childAge is passed to fetchSystemPrompt and client.connect).

FLOW (new friend): START button → handleStartChatting (1277) speaks firstMeetingQuestion → step `first-meeting`. Child answers yes/no to "是不是第一次见面". `yes` (first time) → speak startChattingIntro ("彩虹缤纷镇..." which itself asks "你几岁啦") → step `age`. `no` (returning) → speak OLD_FRIEND_RECOGNITION + agePrompt → step `returning-age`. unclear → re-ask, stay `first-meeting`.
- `age`: parse age → speak weatherPrompt (age-gated: ≤7 drops the snow option, weatherPromptForAge lines 302-304) → step `none` (LLM-driven from the weather answer onward... but actually the weather answer is handled by step `weather-game-choice` only in the `age-short` variant; in the plain `age` path nextStep is `none`, so the WEATHER answer goes to the model). 
- `returning-age`: parse age → speak OLD_FRIEND_AGE_TO_STORY + a random returningSessionIntro daily story (ends with "你今天过得怎么样呢?") → step `returning-intro-answer`. → child replies → mood-mirror (moodIntroAnswerResponse via /api/classify 'mood') + a coin-flip warmup game (sound-detective or rhythm-story) → that game's completion later appends the weather prompt and lands in `weather-game-choice`.

CRITICAL BYPASS (project memory rule): at the start of EVERY scripted branch, `dispatchScriptedOrBypass` (1587-1610) first calls `isActivityIntent(text)` → /api/classify schema 'activity-intent'. If `yes`, it tears down the scripted user-msg/placeholder/apiHistory entry, sets step→`none`, and re-enters sendMessage so the LLM handles the turn. This MUST be preserved on the robot.

SAFETY PRE-EMPTION runs before any scripted step on real user input (1399-1540): /api/risk-assess → high_risk speaks HIGH_RISK_RESPONSE (hotlines) + ends session; concerning speaks CONCERNING_RESPONSE + jumps to `concerning-music-offer`; plus a local keyword filter (safety.distressKeywords). This short-circuits the intro so distress is never swallowed by the yes/no classifier.

INPUT: studio uses a text box + Start/End buttons + a voice-input mic that transcribes (transcribeAudio → /api/transcribe) and feeds the SAME sendMessage. On the robot the text box is gone: key1=START maps to handleStartChatting/handleEndSession, key2=push-to-talk maps to the voice-input recorder → transcribe → sendMessage(text). No typed input exists on the robot.

states.name: session-idle (none, sessionActive=false)
states.purpose: No session running. Face daydreams. Waiting for the START button.
states.enteredWhen: App load, or after handleEndSession (1342-1362) which resets step→'none', sessionActive→false, clears transcript/history/game refs, face→'calm'.
states.onEnter: No audio. Face expression set to 'calm' on end-session; idle/daydream loop runs.

transitions.trigger: START button pressed (studio: 'Start Chatting' button line 3199 → handleStartChatting; robot: key1)
transitions.sideEffects: handleStartChatting (1277): refreshes config (fetchConfig → games/conversationFlow/safety refs), clears transcript+apiHistory+activity state+audio+visemes, resets userTurnCount/breakDue, sessionActive→true, step→'first-meeting'. Speaks getFirstMeetingQuestion() ('我们是第一次见面吗？') via callTts; face→'gentle' then 'calm' 500ms after TTS. Pushes the question into apiHistory as an assistant turn.

states.name: first-meeting
states.purpose: Ask whether this is a first meeting; branch new-friend vs returning-friend.
states.enteredWhen: Set by handleStartChatting (1326), and re-entered on an 'unclear' yes/no answer (1708).
states.onEnter: Already-spoken firstMeetingQuestion (spoken at session start, not re-spoken on entry). On re-ask, speaks the literal '嗯,我没太听明白。你之前有见过我吗?还是这是我们第一次见面?'.

transitions.trigger: Child reply classified by /api/classify schema 'yesno' = 'yes' (IS first meeting). First runs isActivityIntent bypass.
transitions.sideEffects: Speak getStartChattingIntro() (startChattingIntro 'I come from Rainbow Town... 你几岁啦'). face gentle→calm. Append both turns to apiHistory.
Speak OLD_FRIEND_RECOGNITION ('原来我们是老朋友啊！') + '\n\n' + getAgePrompt() ('你今年几岁呀？'). The daily story is deliberately deferred to returning-age.
Speak fixed re-ask line; stay in step.
Remove the user msg + streaming placeholder from transcript, pop the user entry from apiHistory, step→'none', face→'calm', recursive sendMessage(text) so /api/chat handles it.

transitions.trigger: isGoodbyeIntent classified yes AFTER reply spoken (only when step was already 'none'; during intro goodbyePromise is forced false, line 1547-1550)
transitions.sideEffects: During intro this is suppressed; goodbye only ends session once step==='none'.

states.name: age
states.purpose: New-friend age capture, then deliver the weather/mood prompt.
states.enteredWhen: first-meeting 'yes' branch (1704).
states.onEnter: No new line on entry; startChattingIntro (spoken in prior step) already asked the age.

transitions.trigger: Child reply (their age). parseAgeFromText extracts a number; isActivityIntent bypass checked first.
transitions.target: none (LLM takes over from the weather answer)
transitions.sideEffects: If parsed age ∈[1,120]: setChildAge + childAgeRef updated. Speak weatherPromptForAge(getWeatherPrompt(), age) — full prompt for >7, WEATHER_PROMPT_YOUNG (no snow) for ≤7. Append turns to apiHistory. nextStep='none' (NOT age-short): the child's weather answer is then handled by the LLM, not by weather-game-choice. face gentle→calm.
Bypass teardown as in first-meeting; recursive sendMessage.

states.name: age-short
states.purpose: Variant age-capture that routes the weather answer into the scripted game-recommendation funnel instead of the LLM. (Same handler as 'age', isShort=true.)
states.enteredWhen: Not entered during the linear new-friend/returning intro in current code; reserved/used when a shorter weather prompt + scripted weather routing is desired (nextStep='weather-game-choice').
states.onEnter: No spoken line on entry.

transitions.trigger: Child reply with age; isActivityIntent bypass first.
transitions.sideEffects: Parse+store age. Speak getShortWeatherPrompt() ('你觉得哪个天气可以代表你的心情啊？'). step→'weather-game-choice'.

states.name: returning-age
states.purpose: Returning-friend age capture, then play the daily-story intro.
states.enteredWhen: first-meeting 'no' branch (1701).
states.onEnter: Age already asked by the prior step's OLD_FRIEND_RECOGNITION+agePrompt.

transitions.sideEffects: If parsed age∈[1,120]: setChildAge+childAgeRef. Speak OLD_FRIEND_AGE_TO_STORY ('好，那我给你分享一下我今天的故事。') + '\n\n' + a random pickReturningIntroFromConfig() daily story (each ends with '你今天过得怎么样呢？有什么好玩的事吗？'). step→'returning-intro-answer'. face gentle→calm.
Bypass teardown; recursive sendMessage.

states.name: returning-intro-answer
states.purpose: Mood-mirror the child's reply to the daily story, then launch ONE coin-flip warmup game that funnels into the weather/game-recommendation stage.
states.enteredWhen: returning-age (1809).
states.onEnter: No line on entry (the daily story's closing question was spoken in returning-age).

transitions.trigger: Child reply; isActivityIntent bypass first. Then moodIntroAnswerResponse (via /api/classify 'mood') picks a fixed mood-mirror line. Math.random()<0.5 picks branch.
transitions.sideEffects: Sound-detective branch: speak moodReply + getGame2Intro(), waitForEnd, then playSoundEffect(selectedSound.src) via /api/audio/file, then speak the sound's question; step→'game-2-answer'. Rhythm branch: speak moodReply + getGame1Prefix() + a random rhythm story; step→'game-1-completion'. If no sounds configured, fall back: speak moodReply + weather prompt, step→'weather-game-choice'. These warmup/completion steps belong to the GAMES slice; the intro's job ends by handing into them, and they later append the weather prompt → 'weather-game-choice'.
Bypass teardown; recursive sendMessage.

states.name: concerning-music-offer (safety side-entry)
states.purpose: After a 'concerning' risk classification spoke CONCERNING_RESPONSE (which ends '要不要我现在放一段轻柔的音乐给你听?'), capture the yes/no to play comfort music. Listed here because the safety check can fire DURING the intro and pre-empt it.
states.enteredWhen: Safety pre-emption (1532) when /api/risk-assess returns 'concerning' on any user turn (including intro turns).
states.onEnter: CONCERNING_RESPONSE already spoken by the safety block; face 'gentle'.

transitions.trigger: Child reply classified /api/classify 'yesno'. NOTE: dispatchScriptedOrBypass deliberately skipped here (no activity-intent check).
transitions.sideEffects: yes → speak COMFORT_MUSIC_ACCEPTED + play next comfort track (comfortMusicCursor rotation over safety.comfortMusicFiles); else → speak COMFORT_MUSIC_DECLINED. step→'none' either way (LLM resumes).

apiCalls.when: Every intro user reply, to branch. schema='yesno' (first-meeting, concerning-music-offer), schema='activity-intent' (the BYPASS check at the top of every step), schema='mood' (returning-intro-answer mood mirror), schema='goodbye' (only when step==='none'), schema='weather-mood' (weather-game-choice, post-intro), schema='game-name' (game-pick, post-intro).
apiCalls.request: { text: <child utterance>, schema: <ClassifySchema>, context?: <optional> }
apiCalls.responseHandling: Reads { label }. yesno→yes/no/unclear branches. activity-intent→'yes' triggers full bypass to LLM. mood→positive/negative/neutral/unclear selects a fixed mirror sentence. On any fetch error the helpers fail closed (treat as unclear / not-activity-intent / not-goodbye) so the scripted path still runs.

apiCalls.when: Before EVERY scripted step, on real (non-silent, non-override) user input only (lines 1399-1453). Passes the robot's previous assistant line (last 300 chars) as context.
apiCalls.request: { text: <child utterance>, context?: <last robot line, sliced -300> }
apiCalls.responseHandling: Reads { emotion, risk_level }. high_risk → speak HIGH_RISK_RESPONSE (hotlines 12355 / 400-161-9995), do NOT append to apiHistory, then handleEndSession. concerning → speak CONCERNING_RESPONSE, step→'concerning-music-offer', session stays active. safe → emotion maps to a face expression (happy/excited/calm/curious/confused/sad/anxious). On endpoint failure returns {emotion:'neutral',risk_level:'safe'} so a turn is never blocked by a hiccup; a local keyword filter (safety.distressKeywords, bidirectional substring, ≥2 chars) is the second net.

apiCalls.when: Every spoken line in the intro (callTts, line 1158). Style=voiceStyleRef, voice=defaultVoiceRef (zh-CN-XiaoxiaoMultilingualNeural by default).
apiCalls.request: { text: <sanitized server-side>, style, voice }
apiCalls.responseHandling: Returns { audio(base64 mp3), visemes[], ssml }. Studio plays the base64 audio in an HTMLAudioElement and drives the SVG face mouth from visemes via requestAnimationFrame on audio.currentTime. waitForEnd option used when a following action (play sound effect, start comfort music, end session) must not start mid-utterance. ON ROBOT: this drives the LCD face + speaker; lines that are FIXED constants could alternatively be pre-recorded audio, but the studio always synthesizes them through this endpoint.

apiCalls.when: Loaded reactively whenever childAge changes (line 531) and consumed once the LLM takes over (step 'none'). Not used during the scripted intro itself, but the age captured in the intro feeds it.
apiCalls.request: query params configId, childAge (the value parseAgeFromText captured)
apiCalls.responseHandling: Returns { systemPrompt } embedded as the system message for /api/chat. Age bucket affects persona/voice guidance.

apiCalls.when: Voice-input mic path (studio handleVoiceInputClick line 2860). On the robot this is the push-to-talk (key2) path.
apiCalls.request: multipart wav blob
apiCalls.responseHandling: Returns { text, status }. Non-empty text is fed into the SAME sendMessage(text) that the text box uses — so voice and text are interchangeable inputs to the intro state machine. status 'NoMatch' shows a 'didn't catch that' prompt.

apiCalls.when: returning-intro-answer sound-detective warmup plays a sound effect; concerning-music-offer plays a comfort track.
apiCalls.request: path filename (resolveSoundUrl probes mp3/m4a/wav/ogg/aac/flac)
apiCalls.responseHandling: Returned as an Audio src; awaited to completion in the warmup before asking the question.

maps.audioBehavior: In the studio, intro lines are NOT prerecorded — every fixed line (firstMeetingQuestion, startChattingIntro, recognition lines, age prompt, daily story, weather prompt, comfort decline/accept) is synthesized on demand through POST /api/tts/visemes and played as base64 mp3, with visemes driving the mouth. Two genuinely prerecorded/sample audio assets ARE played from /api/audio/file/: the sound-detective warmup sound effect (only on the returning-intro 50% branch) and comfort-music tracks (concerning side-branch, rotated via comfortMusicCursor over safety.comfortMusicFiles — not random). Sequencing rule: when a spoken line must be followed by playing a file or ending the session, callTts is invoked with waitForEnd:true so the file/teardown starts only after the utterance fully ends (e.g. HIGH_RISK_RESPONSE → end session; game-2 intro → play sound → speak question). For the ROBOT, all the FIXED intro constants are strong candidates to be pre-rendered to audio files keyed by their conversationFlow field name (firstMeetingQuestion, startChattingIntro, agePrompt, weatherPrompt + weatherPrompt_young, oldFriendRecognition, oldFriendAgeToStory) and the 10 returningSessionIntros, since they never change per-turn; only the mood-mirror line (4 fixed variants) and the weather/recommendation lines vary by classifier output and can also be pre-rendered as a small fixed set. Anything dynamic (none in the pure intro — the intro emits only fixed strings) would need live TTS.
maps.faceBehavior: Face expression is set imperatively at each phase via setFaceExpr. Pattern per spoken step: on entry/while classifying → 'thinking'; when the resolved reply is set → 'gentle'; 500ms after TTS ends → 'calm' (setTimeout). Session start sets 'gentle' then 'calm'. Safety: high_risk/concerning force 'gentle' during the message then 'calm' after 2000ms. During the safe path, the risk classifier's returned emotion overrides the face before the scripted line is chosen: happy→happy, excited→excited, calm→calm, curious→curious, confused→confused, sad→sad, anxious/scared/angry→anxious (lines 1421-1431). While TTS audio plays, the mouth is driven by the viseme stream from /api/tts/visemes (RAF synced to audio.currentTime); on audio 'ended' the viseme stream clears and face returns to 'calm' after 500ms. Idle/daydream runs when no session and resumes between turns. The robot must reproduce: thinking-while-classifying, gentle-while-speaking, emotion-from-risk-classifier override, calm-at-rest, and viseme-driven mouth during playback (or envelope fallback for prerecorded audio).
maps.buttonRelevance: Studio inputs map to robot's 4 buttons + voice as follows. The studio 'Start Chatting' / 'End Session' buttons (lines 3189-3199) → key1 (START/STOP): press to call handleStartChatting (begins the intro at 'first-meeting'); press again to handleEndSession. The studio text box + Send button (lines 3357-3405) and the voice-input mic both funnel into sendMessage(text) — on the robot there is NO text box, so EVERY child reply during the intro (yes/no to first-meeting, the spoken age, the daily-story reply, the weather word) comes from key2 (push-to-TALK): record → POST /api/transcribe → sendMessage(transcribedText). The intro never needs key3(NEXT)/key4(REPEAT) — those belong to activities — though key4=REPEAT could naturally re-speak the current step's prompt (re-call callTts on the last assistant line) and key3 is unused in the intro. The activity-intent BYPASS still works via voice: if the child says e.g. '我们直接玩音乐吧' into key2, /api/classify 'activity-intent'→yes tears down the scripted turn and hands to the LLM, exactly as in the studio. Age capture works from the transcript string via parseAgeFromText, so a spoken '三岁了' or '我五岁' is parsed identically to typed input.
openQuestions: The plain new-friend 'age' step sets nextStep='none' (line 1741), meaning after the weather prompt the child's WEATHER answer goes to the LLM, NOT into the scripted 'weather-game-choice' game-recommendation funnel. Only the 'age-short' variant (and the returning warmup completion) routes into 'weather-game-choice'. Confirm with the team whether the robot should mirror this asymmetry (new friend → LLM after weather; returning friend → scripted game funnel) or unify it, since it changes which path is fully scripted.

'age-short' step is defined and handled but no transition in the linear intro sets it (only 'age' is reachable from first-meeting 'yes'). Confirm it is intentionally reserved / triggered elsewhere, or dead on the new-friend path, before porting.

childAge default (AGE_DEFAULT) is used if parseAgeFromText returns null or out-of-range; confirm the robot's desired default age bucket when a child gives no parseable age by voice (mis-transcription risk is higher with ASR than typing).

On the robot, should the FIXED intro constants be pre-rendered audio files (faster, no network, deterministic prosody) or still synthesized live via /api/tts/visemes for viseme accuracy? The studio always synthesizes. If pre-rendered, mouth must use the audio-envelope fallback (no viseme events for file playback).

Safety pre-emption calls /api/risk-assess on every turn including intro turns and passes the previous robot line as context — confirm the robot keeps this per-turn risk gate active during the scripted intro (it must, to honor the 'distress never swallowed by yes/no classifier' rule).

goodbyePromise is forced to resolve false while step!=='none' (lines 1547-1550) to stop short affirmatives ('是','嗯','对') from ending the session mid-intro. The robot's voice path must replicate this suppression so an ASR'd 'yes' to first-meeting isn't read as a goodbye.

maps.area: The GAMES slice: the two warmup mini-games — Game 1 rhythm-story (pickGame1Story / completion responses) and Game 2 sound-detective (resolveSoundUrl / pickGame2Sound / isSoundAnswerCorrect via classify 'sound-match'). These are distinct from the 4 numbered activities and from the 4 'recommended games' cards (which are just aliases for the activities).
maps.summary:
CRITICAL TERMINOLOGY — two different things are both called "games" in TestChat.tsx, and they must not be conflated in the Python port:

(A) The TWO REAL WARMUP GAMES — the actual GameConfig union (packages/contracts/src/config.ts lines 162-214): `rhythm-story` (a.k.a. Game 1) and `sound-detective` (a.k.a. Game 2). These are full mini-games with their own audio/scoring. In default.json they live under `games[]` (game-1, game-2, plus a `placeholder` game-3 that is never used at runtime). THIS SLICE = these two.

(B) The FOUR "RECOMMENDED GAMES" — RECOMMENDED_GAMES (TestChat.tsx lines 82-103): rhythm / co-creation / breathing / emotion-mapping. These are NOT GameConfigs. They are display CARDS that alias the four numbered ACTIVITIES (each has an `activityId` like 'body-rhythm'). They are surfaced by the weather→recommend→pick→decide machine, and "starting" one just silently nudges the LLM to call start_activity. The other agent covering activities owns the actual run; I map the pick/decide scaffolding because it is entangled with the warmup games via the weather prompt.

HOW THE REAL WARMUP GAMES (A) ARE REACHED: ONLY in the returning-visitor flow. Step `returning-age` → speaks daily story → sets step `returning-intro-answer`. On the child's reply there (line 1829), `moodIntroAnswerResponse` mirrors mood, then `Math.random() < 0.5` picks sound-detective vs rhythm-story exactly once as a warmup. There is NO standalone "play a game" entry point for these two; they are not in RECOMMENDED_GAMES and the weather machine cannot launch them. After the chosen warmup completes, its handler appends the WEATHER_PROMPT and transitions to `weather-game-choice`, which feeds the recommended-games (B) machine.

GAME 1 — rhythm-story: assistant speaks moodReply + prefix + ONE random story (story instructs the child to clap/tap a rhythm and then SAY "我拍完啦"). State `game-1-completion`. On next child turn, classify('task-completed'): yes → random completionResponse + weather prompt → `weather-game-choice`; no → un-append the turn and hand the raw text to the LLM (scripted flow abandoned). No audio files, no scoring — purely narrated; "completion" is a yes/no AI judgment of whether the child said they finished.

GAME 2 — sound-detective: assistant speaks moodReply + intro, state `game-2-answer`, picks ONE random sound via pickGame2SoundFromConfig (prefers config.games sound with its exact audioFilename → /api/audio/file/<filename>; else hardcoded GAME_2_SOUNDS whose `base` filename is extension-probed by resolveSoundUrl via HEAD requests over mp3/m4a/wav/ogg/aac/flac). Flow: TTS intro (waitForEnd) → playSoundEffect(src) (one-shot, awaits 'ended') → TTS the sound's `question`. On next child turn: isSoundAnswerCorrect = classify('sound-match', text, context=sound.label e.g. "鸡 Chicken") → 'yes'⇒correctResponse, 'no'⇒wrongResponse; lenient grading. Then append weather prompt → `weather-game-choice`. game2SoundRef holds the in-flight sound; cleared after answering.

ROBOT IMPACT: every child input here is a typed text box today, consumed by AI classifiers (task-completed, sound-match) and free-text mood mirroring. On the robot these become push-to-talk (key2) → STT → same classifier text. Sound playback already file-based (good for Pi). The 50/50 warmup pick, story/response random selection, and sound pick are all Math.random and port directly.

states.name: warmup-game-launch (in returning-intro-answer)
states.purpose: Entry point for BOTH real warmup games. After a returning visitor replies to the daily story, mirror their mood then launch exactly ONE warmup mini-game (50/50 rhythm-story vs sound-detective).
states.enteredWhen: scriptedSessionStep === 'returning-intro-answer' and a non-silent child message arrives (set by the prior 'returning-age' step at line 1809). Wrapped in dispatchScriptedOrBypass (activity-intent 'yes' bypasses straight to the LLM, abandoning the warmup).
states.onEnter: Append child msg to transcript+apiHistory; setStreaming(true); face='thinking'. Compute moodReply = await moodIntroAnswerResponse(text,'') (classify schema 'mood' → negative/positive/neutral/unclear → one of 4 fixed Chinese lines). Then useSoundDetective = Math.random() < 0.5.

transitions.trigger: useSoundDetective === true AND pickGame2SoundFromConfig() returns a sound
transitions.sideEffects: game2SoundRef = selectedSound. Speak introBlock = moodReply + '\n\n' + getGame2Intro() (config.games sound-detective.intro, fallback GAME_2_INTRO) via callTts(...,{waitForEnd:true}); face='gentle'. THEN if selectedSound.src: await playSoundEffect(src) (one-shot clip). THEN append + callTts selectedSound.question. Net audio order: TTS intro → sound clip → TTS question.

transitions.trigger: useSoundDetective === true AND pickGame2SoundFromConfig() returns null (no sounds configured)
transitions.sideEffects: Skip the warmup. Speak moodReply + '\n\n' + weatherPromptForAge(getWeatherPrompt(), childAge). face='gentle'.

story = pickGame1StoryFromConfig() (random from config.games rhythm-story.stories, fallback GAME_1_STORIES). Speak fixedReply = moodReply + '

' + getGame1Prefix() + '

' + story via callTts. face='gentle'. (Prefix fallback GAME_1_PREFIX; story text instructs child to clap a rhythm then say '我拍完啦'.)
Remove the just-added child msg, pop apiHistory, step='none', re-send raw text to LLM via sendMessageRef. Warmup skipped entirely.

states.name: game-2-answer (Sound Detective active round)
states.purpose: Child has heard the sound + question and gives a guess; grade it leniently with the AI classifier and respond, then hand off to the weather machine.
states.enteredWhen: scriptedSessionStep === 'game-2-answer' (set during the sound-detective warmup launch). A child message arrives.
states.onEnter: Append child msg; setStreaming(true); face='thinking'. Read selectedSound = game2SoundRef. If null → baseReply = the 'sound got lost' apology line. Else correct = await isSoundAnswerCorrect(text, selectedSound.label) = classify('sound-match', text, context=label); baseReply = correct ? correctResponse : wrongResponse. Wrapped in dispatchScriptedOrBypass (activity-intent yes → bypass to LLM).

transitions.trigger: answer graded (correct or wrong) — always proceeds after grading
transitions.sideEffects: fixedReply = baseReply + '\n\n' + weatherPromptForAge(getWeatherPrompt(), childAge). Append + callTts. game2SoundRef = null. face='gentle' then 'calm' after 500ms. If goodbyePromise resolved true → handleEndSession().
Remove child msg, pop apiHistory, step='none', re-send raw text to LLM.

states.name: game-1-completion (Rhythm Story active round)
states.purpose: Child was told to clap a rhythm then say they're done; detect completion and either close the game or drop the broken scaffolding back to the LLM.
states.enteredWhen: scriptedSessionStep === 'game-1-completion' (set during the rhythm-story warmup launch). A child message arrives.
states.onEnter: Append child msg; setStreaming(true); face='thinking'. completed = await isTaskCompleted(text) = classify('task-completed') → yes/no. Wrapped in dispatchScriptedOrBypass.

transitions.sideEffects: fixedReply = pickGame1CompletionFromConfig() (random completionResponse) + '\n\n' + weatherPromptForAge(getWeatherPrompt(), childAge). Append + callTts. face='gentle'→'calm'.

transitions.trigger: isTaskCompleted === 'no' (still tapping / off-topic / refusing / 我不会 / 再来一次)
transitions.sideEffects: Un-append the child msg from transcript and apiHistory, step='none', setStreaming(false), face='calm', re-send raw text to the LLM via sendMessageRef. Weather step is sacrificed (scripted flow already broken).
Same dispatchScriptedOrBypass bypass as other states.

states.name: weather-game-choice (bridge — NOT a game; leads to recommended-games machine B)
states.purpose: After a warmup game (or directly in the short new-friend flow), the child names a weather standing for their mood; classify it and seed the 'recommended games' pool. This is the seam between real warmup games (A) and the activity-alias cards (B).
states.enteredWhen: scriptedSessionStep === 'weather-game-choice' (set by game-1-completion / game-2-answer onEnter side-effects, or by the short new-friend weather step). Child names a weather.
states.onEnter: Append child msg; face='thinking'. raw = classify('weather-mood') → sunny/cloudy/rainy/snowy/thunder/unclear. Map: sunny→bucket 'sunny'(mirror 晴天); cloudy/rainy/snowy/thunder→bucket 'quiet' (mirror 阴天/下雨天/下雪天/雷雨天); else unclear.

transitions.sideEffects: Speak RE_ASK_WEATHER. Stay in state.

gamePool = [...SUNNY_POOL] = ['rhythm','co-creation']; introducedGames=new Set(); currentGame=null. Speak SUNNY_RECOMMENDATION. (Pool entries are RECOMMENDED_GAMES ids = activity aliases, not real GameConfigs.)
gamePool = [...QUIET_POOL] = ['breathing','emotion-mapping','co-creation']. Speak quietRecommendationLocal(mirror).

states.name: game-pick (recommended-games card chooser — machine B)
states.purpose: Child names one of the recommended games (activity aliases); show its card and ask whether to start it.
states.enteredWhen: scriptedSessionStep === 'game-pick'. Deliberately NOT wrapped in dispatchScriptedOrBypass (bare game names would falsely trip activity-intent and skip the card the child asked to hear).
states.onEnter: game = classify('game-name') → rhythm/co-creation/breathing/emotion-mapping/unclear. face='thinking'.

transitions.sideEffects: Secondary classify('yesno'): if 'yes' (vague affirmation, no game named) → speak RE_ASK_GAME_NAME_VAGUE; else → RE_ASK_GAME_NAME_UNMATCHED. Stay in state.

transitions.trigger: game is one of the 4 ids
transitions.sideEffects: Remove game from gamePool; introducedGames.add(game); currentGame=game. Speak RECOMMENDED_GAMES[game].card + '\n\n' + GAME_DECIDE_PROMPT.

states.name: game-decide (start-or-browse the chosen recommended game — machine B)
states.purpose: After hearing a card the child either starts that activity, names a different one, or asks to browse others.
states.enteredWhen: scriptedSessionStep === 'game-decide'. Not wrapped in bypass (same reason as game-pick).
states.onEnter: Run classify('yesno') and classify('game-name') in PARALLEL (Promise.all). yes/no wins ties so affirmations like '我想尝试一下' aren't misread as a different game.

transitions.trigger: yesno !== 'yes' AND namedGame is a different valid game than currentGame
transitions.sideEffects: Drop namedGame from pool, mark introduced, currentGame=namedGame, speak its card + GAME_DECIDE_PROMPT. Stays in game-decide.

Speak GAME_START_LINE ('好啊,那我们现在就开始咯。'); face='excited'; step='none'. Then sendMessageRef('开始'+RECOMMENDED_GAMES[game].displayName+'吧', {silent:true}) — a SILENT message that makes the LLM call the start_activity tool for the aliased activity. (Hand-off to the activities slice.)

transitions.trigger: yesno !== 'yes' (no/unclear) AND gamePool still has entries
transitions.sideEffects: next = gamePool.shift(); introducedGames.add(next); currentGame=next; speak next card + GAME_DECIDE_PROMPT.
Speak GAME_POOL_EXHAUSTED_PROMPT.

states.name: game-pool-exhausted (recommended games drained — hand to LLM)
states.purpose: All recommended games browsed and declined; drop scripted scaffolding and let the LLM handle whatever the child says next (system prompt will call start_activity on direct intent).
states.enteredWhen: scriptedSessionStep === 'game-pool-exhausted'.
states.onEnter: No classification. Immediately step='none' and re-dispatch the child's text to the LLM via sendMessageRef(text) (non-silent).

transitions.sideEffects: step='none'; sendMessageRef(text). All subsequent turns are free-form LLM.

apiCalls.when: Game 2 (sound-detective) answer check, in state game-2-answer, via isSoundAnswerCorrect(text, label).
apiCalls.request: { text: <child guess>, schema: 'sound-match', context: <sound.label e.g. "鸡 Chicken"> }. Server appends context as 'Expected: <label>' to the system instruction; model temp=0, max_tokens=4.
apiCalls.responseHandling: { label: 'yes' | 'no' }. yes→correctResponse, no→wrongResponse. Grading is intentionally LENIENT (related words / partial matches / animal-family members count as yes). Fails open to 'no' on error (last label).

apiCalls.when: Game 1 (rhythm-story) completion check, state game-1-completion, via isTaskCompleted(text).
apiCalls.request: { text: <child msg>, schema: 'task-completed' } (no context).
apiCalls.responseHandling: { label: 'yes' | 'no' }. yes→random completionResponse + weather prompt → weather-game-choice. no→abandon scripted flow, re-send raw text to LLM. Classifier judges whether the child said they FINISHED tapping (我拍完啦/done) vs still doing it/refusing/我不会.

apiCalls.when: returning-intro-answer warmup launch, via moodIntroAnswerResponse(text,'').
apiCalls.request: { text: <child reply to daily story>, schema: 'mood' }.
apiCalls.responseHandling: label negative/positive/neutral/unclear → one of 4 fixed Chinese mirror lines, prepended to the warmup intro. Fails to 'unclear'.

apiCalls.when: dispatchScriptedOrBypass wrapper around the warmup-launch, game-2-answer, and game-1-completion handlers (NOT game-pick/game-decide).
apiCalls.request: { text, schema: 'activity-intent' }.
apiCalls.responseHandling: label 'yes' → bypass: strip the scripted turn, step='none', re-send raw text to the LLM (child expressed direct intent to do an activity). Anything else → run the scripted reply. Fails closed to false.

apiCalls.when: Recommended-games machine (B): weather-game-choice uses 'weather-mood'; game-pick and game-decide use 'game-name' (game-decide also runs 'yesno' in parallel; game-pick runs 'yesno' as a secondary vague-vs-unmatched discriminator).
apiCalls.request: { text, schema } — no context.
apiCalls.responseHandling: weather-mood → sunny/cloudy/rainy/snowy/thunder/unclear seeds the pool. game-name → rhythm/co-creation/breathing/emotion-mapping/unclear selects a card. These drive activity aliases, not the real warmup games.

apiCalls.when: Every spoken assistant line in all game states (intro, story, question, correct/wrong response, cards, prompts, GAME_START_LINE).
apiCalls.request: { text, style: <voiceStyleRef, default 'cheerful'>, voice?: <defaultVoiceRef> }.
apiCalls.responseHandling: { audio: base64 mp3, visemes:[{audioOffsetMs,visemeId}], ssml }. Browser plays mp3, drives face visemes via RAF clock. {waitForEnd:true} is passed for the Game-2 intro so the sound clip plays AFTER narration. On the robot this maps to Node /api/tts → LCD viseme/expression + speaker.

apiCalls.when: Game 2 sound resolution and playback. Config path: GET on /api/audio/file/<sound.audioFilename> directly (e.g. chicken.m4a, wind(1).mp3). Fallback path: resolveSoundUrl issues HEAD requests probing <base>.{mp3,m4a,wav,ogg,aac,flac} until one returns ok, then GET that URL.
apiCalls.request: HEAD (existence probe, fallback only) / GET (stream, HTTP Range supported).
apiCalls.responseHandling: playSoundEffect(src) creates an Audio element, awaits 'ended' (or 'error'), reusing audioRef so a new turn's stopAudio cleanly cuts it. If autoplay blocked/load error, resolves immediately so the flow continues. Empty src (no file) → playback skipped, flow proceeds straight to the question.

maps.audioBehavior:
Two audio sources, never overlapping (audioRef is shared; stopAudio()/playSoundEffect() pre-empt cleanly):

1) TTS (synthesized) for ALL spoken text — game intros, the rhythm-story story, the sound question, correct/wrong responses, completion responses, weather prompt, recommended-game cards, GAME_START_LINE. Source POST /api/tts/visemes → base64 mp3.

2) PRERECORDED sound-effect clips, ONLY in Game 2 (sound-detective). One clip per round, picked randomly. Selection: pickGame2SoundFromConfig() prefers config.games (sound-detective.sounds[*].audioFilename — exact stored name like 'chicken.m4a', 'wind(1).mp3', 'rain.m4a', 'dog(1).mp3', 'bird.m4a') resolved to /api/audio/file/<name>; falls back to hardcoded GAME_2_SOUNDS whose base name is extension-probed (mp3/m4a/wav/ogg/aac/flac) by resolveSoundUrl via HEAD requests. Played via playSoundEffect (new Audio, awaits 'ended').

Ordering in the Game-2 launch is strict and load-bearing: TTS(intro, waitForEnd:true) → [await clip ends] → TTS(question). The waitForEnd flag exists specifically so the clip is not buried under narration.

Game 1 (rhythm-story) uses NO prerecorded audio at all — it is entirely TTS-narrated; the 'sound' in the story is imaginary and the child produces the rhythm with their own hands.

ROBOT NOTE: clip files already resolve from disk via the Node audio route — directly reusable on the Pi. randomness (Math.random) for clip pick, story pick, completion-response pick, and the 50/50 warmup pick all port verbatim. The browser HEAD-probe fallback is a convenience for un-renamed uploads; the config path (exact audioFilename) is the production path and what the robot should rely on.
maps.faceBehavior:
Face is driven by setFaceExpr() calls plus the viseme stream from /api/tts/visemes (mouth follows audio via a RAF clock on audio.currentTime). Per-state expression choreography in the games:

- Every game handler on entry: setFaceExpr('thinking') while classifying/streaming.
- After producing a spoken reply: setFaceExpr('gentle') during playback, then setTimeout(()=>setFaceExpr('calm'),500) after audio 'ended'. callTts also force-resets face to 'calm' 500ms after each utterance ends and clears the viseme stream.
- game-decide 'yes' (start the game): setFaceExpr('excited') while speaking GAME_START_LINE (the one upbeat moment), then 'calm'.
- No game-specific expression beyond thinking→gentle→calm, except the excited start beat. The 16-expression library is otherwise exercised by the activities, not these warmup games.

ROBOT MAPPING: replace setFaceExpr(id) with renderer.set_expression(id) on the LCD; replace the browser viseme-RAF lip-sync with the robot's audio-envelope/viseme mouth drive already in face/renderer.py. The 500ms-to-calm idle handoff matches the daydream-resume rule (idle resumes ~500ms after last viseme).
maps.buttonRelevance:
Today every transition in this slice is triggered by FREE-FORM TYPED TEXT in the studio text box, then interpreted by AI classifiers — NOT by discrete choices. This is the main robot-port gap. Mapping to the 4 buttons + voice (key1=START/STOP, key2=push-to-TALK, key3=NEXT, key4=REPEAT):

- Game 1 (rhythm-story) completion '我拍完啦': naturally a key3=NEXT ('I'm done') press OR a key2 voice utterance routed through classify('task-completed'). NEXT is the cleaner robot affordance — the child taps a rhythm with their hands (the whole point), then presses NEXT. The voice path must remain for kids who speak instead.

- Game 2 (sound-detective) guess: inherently OPEN-ENDED ('what made this sound?') — there is no fixed button set, so this REQUIRES key2 voice → STT → classify('sound-match'). Buttons cannot replace it. key4=REPEAT should re-play the sound clip (re-call playSoundEffect on the same game2SoundRef.src) — a natural fit since kids will want to hear it again.

- weather-game-choice / game-pick / game-decide (machine B): these present small fixed choice sets (5 weathers; 2-3 game cards; yes/look-at-others). On the robot, key3=NEXT could cycle options and key2 voice still works; game-decide's yes/no maps to key1/NEXT or voice. But this is the activity-alias machine, owned by the activities slice — flagged here only because the warmup games exit into it.

- key1=STOP at any point should abort: clear game2SoundRef, stop audio, reset scriptedSessionStep to a safe state (mirrors handleEndSession / stopAudio).

General rule for the port: each classify() call is a 'turn the child's voice into one of N labels' step — the Python runtime should capture mic on key2, STT via /api/transcribe, then call /api/classify with the same schema/context. Discrete buttons are an additional fast path only where the choice set is fixed (NEXT=done in Game 1, REPEAT=replay in Game 2).
openQuestions: The two REAL warmup games (rhythm-story, sound-detective) are reachable ONLY through the returning-visitor branch (returning-age → returning-intro-answer → 50/50 launch). New-friend / short flows never play them; they jump straight to weather → recommended-activity cards. Is that intended on the robot, or should the warmup games get their own button-triggered entry point? The 4 GPIO buttons have no current mapping to 'launch a warmup game'.

placeholder game-3 (kind 'placeholder', empty notes) exists in games[] but is never instantiated at runtime — getSoundDetective/getRhythmStory only match by kind. Safe for the Python port to ignore placeholder GameConfigs entirely.

Game 1 'no' branch (isTaskCompleted=no) silently abandons the scripted weather hand-off and dumps the child into free LLM chat, losing the weather→recommendation funnel. On the robot with no text box, a key3=NEXT press could be treated as definitive completion to avoid this lossy classifier path — confirm whether NEXT should hard-skip the task-completed classifier.

resolveSoundUrl's HEAD-probe extension fallback (mp3/m4a/wav/ogg/aac/flac) is browser-fetch behavior for un-renamed uploads. On the Pi the published bundle's audioManifest references files by exact filename + sha256, so the probe is likely unnecessary — confirm the robot resolves Game-2 clips straight from the manifest by audioFilename rather than re-probing extensions.
Game 2 has no explicit REPEAT today (the studio just shows text). key4=REPEAT replaying the sound clip is a proposed robot affordance not present in the source — confirm desired.

childAge influences only the weather PROMPT wording (weatherPromptForAge drops the snow option for age≤7), not the games themselves; story/sound selection is age-independent. Confirm the robot should keep games age-agnostic.

maps.area: SAFETY/DISTRESS short-circuit + EMOTION routing (port of TestChat.tsx safety pipeline + emotion→expression mapping to the Pi Python runtime)
maps.summary:
Every real (non-silent, non-override) user turn runs a two-layer safety gate at the TOP of sendMessage (TestChat.tsx ~L1377-1496), BEFORE the scripted-intro state machine, the quit-activity check, and the LLM. Layer 1 is the server AI classifier POST /api/risk-assess (risk.ts) returning {emotion, risk_level} where risk_level ∈ safe|concerning|high_risk. Layer 2 is a LOCAL bidirectional-substring keyword match against safety.distressKeywords, run only when Layer 1 did not already say high_risk.

Three outcomes:
- high_risk (Layer 1) OR keyword hit (Layer 2) → safetyBlocked: tear down audio/visemes, endActivity(), append user msg + fixed HIGH_RISK_RESPONSE (trusted-adult + hotlines 12355 / 400-161-9995) to the VISIBLE transcript only (NOT apiHistory — the LLM never sees distress text, avoiding Azure content-filter), face gentle→calm, speak via TTS with waitForEnd:true, then handleEndSession() (ENDS the session).
- concerning (Layer 1, no keyword hit) → short-circuit BEFORE scripted/LLM, append fixed CONCERNING_RESPONSE (comfort + steer-to-adult + offer soft music), face gentle→calm, NOT in apiHistory, set scriptedSessionStep='concerning-music-offer', session STAYS active. Note: the in-code comment claims this calls the LLM with concerningMode:true — that is STALE; the actual code is a fixed client-side reply. concerningMode is never sent to /api/chat.
- safe → fall through; the classifier's `emotion` drives the face via a HARDCODED switch (L1421-1430), then normal flow continues.

The 'concerning-music-offer' step (next turn, L1618-1665) classifies the child's yes/no via /api/classify schema 'yesno'; yes → speak COMFORT_MUSIC_ACCEPTED then play next safety.comfortMusicFiles track (module-level round-robin cursor comfortMusicCursor); no/unclear → COMFORT_MUSIC_DECLINED; either way reset step→'none'.

EMOTION routing has TWO independent paths, NEITHER reads config.emotionRouting: (1) the risk classifier emotion → expression via a hardcoded switch on safe turns; (2) the server chat stream emits an onExpression timeline (clause-sentiment) and TestChat picks the highest-confidence ExpressionEvent → setFaceExpr. config.emotionRouting (10 routes: joy→happy, fear→gentle, anger→calm, etc.) and safety.assistantDistressMarkers are DEAD config in the studio runtime — not referenced anywhere in TestChat. assistantDistressMarkers was the old post-assistant 'assistant-distress' re-classification layer, explicitly REMOVED (comment L2525-2530).

states.name: SAFETY_GATE
states.purpose: Two-layer distress screen run at the top of every real user turn before any other dispatch.
states.enteredWhen: sendMessage(text) is called with a non-empty text, session active, and NOT opts.silent and NOT an overrideText (i.e. genuine child input, not a silent '继续' auto-advance or a programmatic bypass re-entry).
states.onEnter: cancelAutoAdvance(); pauseComfortMusic() (setComfortMusic(null) → stops any playing comfort track). Compute lastRobotLine = last assistant entry in apiHistory, sliced to trailing 300 chars, as classifier context. Call assessUserRisk(text, lastRobotLine) → POST /api/risk-assess {text, context}. Then (if not already high_risk) run local keyword match over safety.distressKeywords.

transitions.sideEffects: set safetyBlocked=true; skip the emotion-face update (a blocked turn forces its own expression).
set concerningModeForThisTurn=true; drive face from emotion via hardcoded switch BEFORE short-circuit.

transitions.trigger: risk_level !== high_risk AND a keyword in safety.distressKeywords matches text (bidirectional substring: forward lowered.includes(kw); reverse kw.includes(lowered) when lowered.length>=2; kw must be >=2 chars)
transitions.sideEffects: set safetyBlocked=true.

Map classifier emotion → ExpressionId via hardcoded switch (happy→happy, excited→excited, calm→calm, curious→curious, confused→confused, sad→sad, anxious|scared|angry→anxious, else null) and setFaceExpr if non-null. Then proceed to goodbye-intent classify, quit-activity check, scripted-intro state machine, or LLM.

Caught and swallowed (fail-safe): classifier result ignored, but the Layer-2 keyword filter STILL runs. assessUserRisk client wrapper also returns {emotion:'neutral',risk_level:'safe'} on non-OK HTTP so a bad endpoint degrades to keyword-only.

states.name: BLOCKED_CRISIS
states.purpose: Deterministic crisis-redirect: speak fixed hotline/trusted-adult message, then END the session. Distress text never reaches the cloud LLM.
states.enteredWhen: safetyBlocked === true (high_risk from Layer 1 OR keyword hit from Layer 2).
states.onEnter: cancelRef cancel; pause+null audioRef; cancel viseme RAF; clear viseme stream; if an activity is running call endActivity(). Append to VISIBLE transcript only: {role:'user', content:text} and {role:'assistant', content:HIGH_RISK_RESPONSE}. Do NOT append to apiHistory. setStreaming(false); setFaceExpr('gentle'). Speak: await callTts(HIGH_RISK_RESPONSE, id, {waitForEnd:true}) (waitForEnd REQUIRED so endSession's audio.pause doesn't cut it off). HIGH_RISK_RESPONSE = the constant at L131 (你心里特别难受/想伤害自己 → run to a trusted adult + hotlines 12355 青少年心理咨询热线 and 400-161-9995 希望24热线). NOTE: the studio intentionally does NOT raise the red-highlight distressTrigger flag or the caregiver banner here (operator request — ending the session is signal enough). safety.distressResponseScript and distressCaregiverNote exist in config but are NOT used by this path.

transitions.sideEffects: setTimeout(()=>setFaceExpr('calm'),2000); handleEndSession() → cancel streams, setComfortMusic(null), endActivity(), setSessionActive(false), reset scriptedSessionStep='none', clear game/pool refs, setFaceExpr('calm'), userTurnCount=0, breakDue=false. return.

states.name: CONCERNING_RESPONSE_STATE
states.purpose: Deterministic comfort reply for distress-but-not-crisis; offers soft music; session stays alive.
states.enteredWhen: concerningModeForThisTurn === true (risk_level concerning, no keyword hit).
states.onEnter: cancelRef cancel; pause+null audioRef; cancel viseme RAF; clear viseme stream; if activity running endActivity(). Append to VISIBLE transcript only: {role:'user',content:text} and {role:'assistant',content:CONCERNING_RESPONSE}. Do NOT append to apiHistory. setStreaming(false); setFaceExpr('gentle'). Set scriptedSessionStep='concerning-music-offer' (and ref) BEFORE TTS resolves so a fast reply is routed right. Speak: await callTts(CONCERNING_RESPONSE, id) (no waitForEnd). CONCERNING_RESPONSE = constant at L139 (听起来你现在心里一定很沉重吧 ... 要不要我现在放一段轻柔的音乐给你听?).

transitions.sideEffects: setTimeout(()=>setFaceExpr('calm'),2000); return. Session remains active; next user turn is handled by the concerning-music-offer step.

states.name: CONCERNING_MUSIC_OFFER
states.purpose: Resolve the child's yes/no to the soft-music offer; play a comfort track or acknowledge decline; then return to normal.
states.enteredWhen: Next real user turn while scriptedSessionStepRef === 'concerning-music-offer'. Bypasses the dispatchScriptedOrBypass activity-intent helper on purpose (child just heard a distress reply).
states.onEnter: Append {role:'user',content:text} to visible transcript; setStreaming(true); setFaceExpr('thinking'). Classify: classifyIntent(text,'yesno') → /api/classify schema 'yesno' (labels yes|no|unclear). accepted = (label==='yes'). reply = accepted ? COMFORT_MUSIC_ACCEPTED : COMFORT_MUSIC_DECLINED. Append reply as assistant msg; reset step→'none'; setFaceExpr('gentle'); await callTts(reply, id, {waitForEnd:true}). If accepted: pick safety.comfortMusicFiles[comfortMusicCursor % len] (module-level cursor, ++ and wrap), log, setComfortMusic({filename, paused:false}) which a useEffect turns into an <audio> element that plays and self-clears on 'ended'. If no files configured, console.warn only.

transitions.sideEffects: Speak COMFORT_MUSIC_ACCEPTED, then start next comfort-music track via setComfortMusic; step reset to 'none'.

transitions.trigger: yesno classify resolves (no/unclear) or classify throws
transitions.sideEffects: Speak COMFORT_MUSIC_DECLINED; no music; step reset to 'none'. (catch defaults yesno='unclear'.)

states.name: NORMAL_FLOW
states.purpose: Non-distress dispatch — goodbye check, quit-activity check, scripted-intro state machine, or LLM call. Out of this slice's scope except where it drives emotion expressions.
states.enteredWhen: Safety gate returned safe and no concerning/blocked short-circuit fired, OR after the concerning-music-offer resolves.
states.onEnter: On safe turns the classifier emotion already set the face. Subsequent expression changes come from per-step hardcoded setFaceExpr calls (thinking while classifying, gentle after a scripted reply, excited on a positive game pick, calm at rest) and from the LLM chat stream's onExpression timeline.

transitions.trigger: Server chat stream emits an 'expression' event (clause-sentiment timeline)
transitions.sideEffects: onExpression(timeline): pick the ExpressionEvent with highest confidence; setFaceExpr(best.expressionId). This is the SECOND, independent emotion→expression path and also ignores config.emotionRouting.

transitions.trigger: isGoodbyeIntent(text) (classify schema 'goodbye') resolves yes after the assistant TTS finishes
transitions.sideEffects: handleEndSession(). Only armed when scriptedSessionStep==='none'.

states.name: SESSION_ENDED
states.purpose: Terminal: session torn down after crisis block or goodbye.
states.enteredWhen: handleEndSession() called.
states.onEnter: All audio/streams cancelled, comfort music cleared, activity ended, session inactive, scriptedSessionStep='none', counters reset, face 'calm'.

apiCalls.when: FIRST thing on every real (non-silent, non-override) user turn — Layer 1 of the safety gate.
apiCalls.request: { text: <child utterance, 1..2000 chars>, context?: <robot's previous line, last assistant content sliced to trailing 300 chars> }. Server (risk.ts) calls Azure Foundry chat (temperature 0, max_tokens 60, JSON mode) with INSTRUCTION (the long classifier prompt incl. clause (d) passive-ideation override) plus an appended CONTEXT note when context is present (context may disambiguate a vague reply but can NEVER lower a self-harm/death/violence signal).
apiCalls.responseHandling: { emotion: one of happy|sad|angry|scared|anxious|excited|calm|confused|curious|neutral, risk_level: safe|concerning|high_risk }. Dispatch: high_risk→BLOCKED_CRISIS (safetyBlocked); concerning→CONCERNING_RESPONSE_STATE; safe→emotion drives face via hardcoded switch then continue. Server fails CLOSED on an Azure content-filter block (self_harm/violence filtered → high_risk; sexual/hate filtered → null/safe to avoid false positives on '3岁'; unknown category → concerning), and fails SAFE (neutral/safe) on malformed JSON or network/auth/timeout. Client wrapper also returns {neutral,safe} on any non-OK HTTP.

apiCalls.when: In CONCERNING_MUSIC_OFFER, to read the child's answer to '要不要放一段轻柔的音乐?'.
apiCalls.request: { text:<child reply>, schema:'yesno' }. Server returns label ∈ yes|no|unclear (single-token classify, temp 0, max_tokens 4, fail-open to last label 'unclear').
apiCalls.responseHandling: yes → play comfort track (round-robin safety.comfortMusicFiles) + speak COMFORT_MUSIC_ACCEPTED; no|unclear → speak COMFORT_MUSIC_DECLINED. Step reset to 'none' either way.

apiCalls.when: On normal turns (scriptedSessionStep==='none'), kicked off in parallel; consumed after assistant TTS finishes to decide whether to end the session.
apiCalls.request: { text, schema:'goodbye' } → label yes|no. Conservative: only EXPLICIT farewells = yes; acks/quits/mood replies = no.
apiCalls.responseHandling: yes → handleEndSession() (terminal). Relevant here because a distress turn never reaches this (it short-circuits earlier).

apiCalls.when: Normal/safe free-form turns only. Listed to record what the slice does NOT do.
apiCalls.request: Distress turns (high_risk/concerning) are NEVER sent here — distress text is kept out of apiHistory. The in-code comment about sending concerningMode:true is STALE; no concerningMode field is ever sent.
apiCalls.responseHandling: The stream emits 'expression' timeline events (clause-sentiment) consumed by onExpression → highest-confidence ExpressionEvent → setFaceExpr. This is a second emotion path that also ignores config.emotionRouting. The old post-assistant 'assistant-distress' re-classification (schema exists in classify.ts) is REMOVED from the runtime.

apiCalls.when: Never called by TestChat. Documented because safety.assistantDistressMarkers config and this schema were the OLD second safety layer (re-classify the assistant reply, end session if distress). The Python port should decide whether to revive it; today it is unused.
apiCalls.request: { text:<assistant message>, schema:'assistant-distress' } → yes|no.
apiCalls.responseHandling: N/A in current runtime.

maps.audioBehavior: Prerecorded comfort music: only after the CONCERNING path and an accepted offer. Files come from safety.comfortMusicFiles (default: 柔和音乐.mp3, 柔和音乐2.mp3), served from /api/audio/file/<filename>. Selection is a module-level ROUND-ROBIN cursor `comfortMusicCursor` (++ then % files.length) — deliberately NOT random (operator saw Math.random always land on the same file); the cursor persists across the session/module so successive accepts alternate tracks. On the Pi this becomes: maintain a persistent index, play comfortMusicFiles[idx % len] from the local audio cache, advance idx. The track stops the moment the child engages again (pauseComfortMusic→setComfortMusic(null) at the top of the next sendMessage) and self-clears on natural end. The two fixed safety responses (HIGH_RISK_RESPONSE, CONCERNING_RESPONSE) and the two offer follow-ups (COMFORT_MUSIC_ACCEPTED/DECLINED) are NOT prerecorded clips — they are TTS'd live via callTts (POST /api/tts). For the robot these four fixed strings are strong candidates to pre-render to static audio files so a crisis reply never depends on a live TTS round-trip. high_risk TTS uses waitForEnd:true (must finish before endSession cuts audio); the accepted-offer intro line also uses waitForEnd:true so music starts AFTER the spoken line, not over it.
maps.faceBehavior: Two independent emotion→expression drivers, BOTH ignore config.emotionRouting (which is therefore dead config on the robot too unless the port wires it): (1) Safe-turn risk emotion: a hardcoded switch maps the 10 risk-classifier emotion labels → ExpressionId: happy→happy, excited→excited, calm→calm, curious→curious, confused→confused, sad→sad, anxious|scared|angry→anxious, anything else→no change. (2) LLM chat stream onExpression timeline (server clause-sentiment): pick the ExpressionEvent with max confidence → setFaceExpr. Distress-path expression choreography is fixed, not emotion-derived: BLOCKED_CRISIS sets gentle during the spoken hotline message, then calm after 2000ms (post-TTS). CONCERNING sets gentle, then calm after 2000ms. The music-offer turn goes thinking (while classifying yes/no) → gentle (speaking reply) → calm after 1000ms. A blocked turn explicitly does NOT apply the emotion switch. Important divergence to flag for the port: config.emotionRouting uses labels joy/excitement/sadness/fear/anger/curiosity/tiredness/surprise/pride/neutral with targets like fear→gentle, anger→calm, tiredness→sleepy, pride→proud, surprise→surprised — but the risk classifier emits a DIFFERENT label set (happy/scared/angry/...), and the hardcoded switch covers only 7 of them and never reads the config table. The Python port should pick ONE source of truth (recommend: drive the face from config.emotionRouting by mapping the classifier labels onto it, or keep the hardcoded switch but document the gap).
maps.buttonRelevance: This slice is almost entirely input-agnostic — the safety gate, classifiers, and emotion routing all operate on a finalized utterance string, so on the robot the text comes from the push-to-TALK (key2) voice path (ASR → /api/transcribe → text) instead of the studio's text box. Key mapping for the port: (a) The safety gate runs on the ASR transcript of every key2 voice turn that is NOT a silent system advance — preserve the opts.silent / overrideText exclusion (NEXT=key3 '继续' auto-advances and programmatic bypass re-entries must SKIP the gate, exactly as today). (b) The 'concerning-music-offer' yes/no, currently typed, must become a voice yes/no (key2 → ASR → classify 'yesno'); a button shortcut could also map key3/NEXT≈accept music or a dedicated yes/no, but the faithful port keeps it voice-classified. (c) HIGH_RISK end-of-session is automatic (no input) — afterward the robot returns to idle until key1/START. (d) REPEAT (key4) should be able to replay the last fixed safety line / the comfort track. (e) STOP (key1) maps to handleEndSession's teardown (stop audio, clear comfort music, face→calm). No part of this slice requires a keyboard; the only text-box assumption is that input arrives as a string, which the voice pipeline already satisfies.
openQuestions: config.emotionRouting (10 routes incl. fear→gentle, anger→calm, tiredness→sleepy, pride→proud, surprise→surprised, neutral→calm) is COMPLETELY UNUSED by TestChat — the runtime uses a hardcoded 7-label switch instead, and the label sets don't even match (config 'joy/anger/fear' vs classifier 'happy/angry/scared'). Should the Python robot honor config.emotionRouting as the source of truth, or replicate the hardcoded switch verbatim? This is a studio↔robot contract decision.

safety.assistantDistressMarkers and safety.distressResponseScript and safety.distressCaregiverNote are config fields that the studio runtime does NOT consume (the assistant-distress re-classification layer was removed; the spoken crisis line is the hardcoded HIGH_RISK_RESPONSE constant, not distressResponseScript). Should the robot (a) keep the hardcoded constants, or (b) actually read these config fields so the Safety panel edits take effect? Today editing them in the studio changes nothing at runtime.

The in-code comment for the 'concerning' path describes calling the LLM with concerningMode:true (strip activity tools, inject a steer-to-adult system note). The ACTUAL code does no such thing — it speaks a fixed CONCERNING_RESPONSE and offers music, never calling /api/chat. Which behavior should the robot implement: the documented LLM-with-concerningMode, or the shipped fixed-response? (Server /api/chat has no concerningMode param today.)
The crisis hotlines (12355, 400-161-9995) are hardcoded Mandarin-specific numbers inside HIGH_RISK_RESPONSE. Confirm these are correct/desired for the robot's deployment region before shipping.

high_risk ENDS the session with no caregiver banner / no escalation signal (operator chose this for the studio). On a physical robot with no screen-watching operator, is silently ending acceptable, or does the robot need a distinct caregiver alert (LED pattern / sound / network ping)? CLAUDE.md lists distress escalation as cut from v1, but the physical context differs.
comfortMusicCursor is a module-level variable persisting across sessions in the studio. On the robot, decide its lifetime (per-session vs persistent) and where it lives in the Python runtime.

Both emotion paths can fight: the safe-turn risk emotion sets the face immediately, then ~1-3s later the chat stream's onExpression overrides it. Confirm the desired precedence/ordering on the robot, especially since voice-mode latency differs from the studio.

maps.area: Embodiment plumbing — audio playback, face drive (expression + viseme lip-sync), TTS, chat streaming, and voice capture/transcription. This is the layer the Python embodiment (robot/face/renderer.py + an audio/mic layer) replaces.
maps.summary:
TestChat.tsx is the orchestration brain; the EMBODIMENT layer underneath it has five concrete sub-systems the Python port must reproduce 1:1.

1) TTS + VISEME LIP-SYNC (callTts, TestChat.tsx:1158-1273). Every scripted/LLM line is spoken via `callTts(text, msgId, {waitForEnd?})`. It calls `fetchTtsVisemes(text, voiceStyle, defaultVoice)` → POST /api/tts/visemes → returns `{ audio: base64 mp3, visemes: [{audioOffsetMs, visemeId}], ssml }`. It then: `setVisemeStream(data.visemes)`, `setVisemePlaybackMs(0)`, creates `new Audio('data:audio/mpeg;base64,'+data.audio)`, and on each rAF tick writes `setVisemePlaybackMs(round(audio.currentTime*1000))`. The SVG face reads visemeStream + visemePlaybackMs and snaps the mouth. On `ended`: stop rAF, `setVisemePlaybackMs(-1)`, after 500ms clear visemes + `setFaceExpr('calm')`. `callTts` is gated by `mutedRef`. Lip-sync = audio playback clock (currentTime) used to index a pre-fetched viseme timeline; NO realtime viseme stream in scripted mode.

2) FACE DRIVE. The face is driven by ONE state var `faceExpr` (ExpressionId), set imperatively via `setFaceExpr(...)` at ~60 call sites (listening when user speaks/records, thinking while waiting on LLM, gentle/excited/happy before speaking a line, calm 500-2000ms after). NOTE: TestChat does NOT feed `expressionTimeline` to the renderer (only the Face.tsx demo does). The server DOES stream an expression timeline (onExpression), but the panel collapses it: `best = timeline.reduce(max confidence)` then `setFaceExpr(best.expressionId)`. So on the robot the face is a single current-expression value plus the viseme mouth override — not a scheduled timeline. Viseme mouth (when visemePlaybackMs>=0) suppresses idle and overrides mouth shape; expression still drives eyes/color.

3) CHAT STREAMING (startChatStream, chatStream.ts). POST /api/chat SSE; events: `text` (delta → append to bubble + streamingContentRef), `expression` (timeline → setFaceExpr best), `tool_call` (start_activity/play_melody/end_activity → activity+playlist state), `done` (finalize, then callTts(finalContent), schedule auto-advance). TTS is fired in onDone AFTER the full text streams — text is NOT spoken incrementally; the whole assistant turn is synthesized in one /api/tts/visemes call.

4) AUDIO PLAYBACK CHANNELS — three independent HTMLAudio elements: (a) `audioRef` = TTS voice + one-shot game sound effects (mutually exclusive, stopAudio() pre-empts); (b) `activityAudioRef` = activity background-music playlist (looping/fading, co-creation replays via playCount, emotion-mapping uses a 20s/section rAF fade timer); (c) `comfortMusicAudioRef` = soft track after distress. All resolve files via `/api/audio/file/:filename` (resolveSoundUrl probes mp3/m4a/wav/ogg/aac/flac via HEAD). Volume/fade done by mutating audio.volume in rAF loops.

5) VOICE INPUT — TWO independent paths. (a) One-shot push-to-talk in text mode: `VoiceInputRecorder` (16kHz, mic-worklet, RMS VAD: speech>0.02, 800ms trailing silence ends, 4s no-speech cancel, 15s cap) → WAV blob → `transcribeAudio(blob,'zh-CN')` → POST /api/transcribe → text → sendMessage(text). (b) Continuous Voice Live: `VoiceLiveClient` opens WS /api/voice-live, streams 24kHz PCM16 via mic-worklet, plays returned PCM16 via VoiceLivePlayback (gapless 24kHz scheduler), forwards realtime viseme + expression + transcript events. Both use AudioWorklet only (mic-worklet.js). The robot replaces these with its mic + Pi-side STT calling /api/transcribe (or the Voice Live WS).

The robot must reproduce: /api/tts/visemes call + audio playback clock-driven viseme indexing for LCD lip-sync; single faceExpr + viseme-mouth-override drawing; /api/chat SSE consumption with onDone→TTS sequencing; three separate audio channels with volume fades; mic VAD capture → /api/transcribe (or Voice Live WS). The text box is replaced by the 4 buttons + mic, but every code path ultimately calls `sendMessage(text)` / `callTts(text)` / `setFaceExpr(id)` — those are the seams the Python port hooks.

states.name: FaceIdle
states.purpose: Daydream face when not speaking/streaming/recording. Single faceExpr='calm' with idle behavior (blink/breath/drift) running.
states.enteredWhen: No audio playing AND not streaming: in JSX idleEnabled = !streaming && visemePlaybackMs < 0. After any TTS ends, a 500-2000ms timeout calls setFaceExpr('calm').
states.onEnter: setFaceExpr('calm'); visemeStream cleared (after 500ms post-TTS); idle loop (blink 3-5s, breath sway, eye drift) active in renderer.

transitions.trigger: User starts speaking/recording (handlePttStart, onSpeechStart, voice-input onStart) OR sendMessage begins
transitions.sideEffects: setFaceExpr('listening')

transitions.trigger: A scripted line or LLM turn is about to be spoken
transitions.sideEffects: setFaceExpr('gentle'|'excited'|'happy'|...) then callTts()

states.name: FaceListening
states.purpose: Face shows it is attending to the child while mic captures / before LLM responds.
states.enteredWhen: sendMessage() entry sets setFaceExpr('listening'); PTT start; Voice Live onSpeechStart; one-shot recorder onStart.
states.onEnter: setFaceExpr('listening'). For one-shot/Voice Live: mic capture running (AudioWorklet PCM16, RMS metered).

transitions.sideEffects: setFaceExpr('thinking')

transitions.trigger: Risk classifier returns an emotion for the user turn
transitions.sideEffects: setFaceExpr mapped from emotion (happy/excited/calm/curious/confused/sad/anxious) — see emotion→expression map at TestChat.tsx:1422

transitions.trigger: VAD auto-stop (800ms silence) / PTT release / no-speech-4s
transitions.sideEffects: recorder.finish→WAV→transcribeAudio; or VoiceLive commit

states.name: FaceThinking
states.purpose: Waiting on the Node brain (chat SSE) or transcription.
states.enteredWhen: Right before/while awaiting startChatStream or transcribeAudio response.
states.onEnter: setFaceExpr('thinking'). streamingContentRef reset. Assistant bubble created with streaming:true.

transitions.sideEffects: setFaceExpr(best-confidence expressionId from streamed timeline)
finalize bubble; setTimeout setFaceExpr('calm',2000); callTts(finalContent); schedule auto-advance
sendMessageRef.current(text) — re-enters the pipeline

states.name: FaceSpeaking
states.purpose: TTS audio playing; mouth lip-syncs to viseme stream; expression set to a warm pose.
states.enteredWhen: callTts() runs: fetchTtsVisemes resolves, audio.play() starts.
states.onEnter: setFaceExpr(gentle/excited/happy/etc) set by caller BEFORE callTts; setVisemeStream(data.visemes); setVisemePlaybackMs(0); play data-URI mp3; rAF loop writes visemePlaybackMs=audio.currentTime*1000 each frame; idle suppressed (visemePlaybackMs>=0).

transitions.sideEffects: cancel rAF; setVisemePlaybackMs(-1); after 500ms setVisemeStream([]) + setFaceExpr('calm'); flushPendingMelody if queued; schedule activity auto-advance ('继续' silent) or end-activity
audio.pause(); audioRef=null; cancel rAF; setVisemePlaybackMs(-1)
caller blocks on playbackEnded promise before continuing scripted sequence

states.name: VoiceLiveActive
states.purpose: Continuous duplex voice mode (alternative to one-shot). Separate from the text/one-shot path. Robot may prefer the simpler one-shot /api/transcribe path instead.
states.enteredWhen: voiceMode toggled on: new VoiceLiveClient + client.connect(childAge). PTT (space/button) starts recording.
states.onEnter: WS /api/voice-live opened; waits xi.ready→setVoiceReady. startRecording() streams 24kHz PCM16. Incoming response.audio.delta played gapless via VoiceLivePlayback; onAudioFrame→setVisemePlaybackMs(playbackMs); response.animation_viseme.delta→append to visemeStream (sorted); xi.expression→setFaceExpr; transcripts→transcript.

transitions.sideEffects: setFaceExpr('listening'); create streaming assistant bubble; setVisemeStream([]); setVisemePlaybackMs(0)
finalize bubble; setStreaming(false); setTimeout setFaceExpr('calm',2000)
client.disconnect(); stopAudio(); on error setFaceExpr('confused')

apiCalls.when: Every spoken line — both scripted constants (callTts) and the full LLM turn (onDone). Body {text, style, voice?}.
apiCalls.request: fetchTtsVisemes(text, voiceStyleRef.current (e.g. 'cheerful'), defaultVoiceRef.current). client.ts:99-111.
apiCalls.responseHandling: Returns {audio: base64 mp3, visemes:[{audioOffsetMs,visemeId}], ssml}. Play audio as data:audio/mpeg;base64 element; drive mouth by indexing visemes with audio.currentTime. PYTHON: call this, decode mp3, play on speaker, and use the playback position to index the viseme list for LCD mouth shapes (visemeMap).

apiCalls.when: Every real LLM turn (not scripted-intro/game branches, which are local constants). startChatStream, TestChat.tsx:2420.
apiCalls.request: {configId:'default', childAge, messages: full apiHistory, activityContext?, concerningMode?}. Streamed via fetch+ReadableStream (NOT EventSource — POST).
apiCalls.responseHandling: SSE events parsed in chatStream.ts: type 'text'(delta), 'expression'(timeline[]), 'tool_call'(name+args+result), 'done'(usage), 'error'. onText appends bubble; onExpression→setFaceExpr; onToolCall→activity/playlist; onDone→callTts(fullText)+auto-advance. PYTHON: parse SSE the same way; speak only after 'done'.

apiCalls.when: One-shot voice input path: after VoiceInputRecorder VAD-stops and yields a WAV blob.
apiCalls.request: transcribeAudio(wavBlob,'zh-CN') — multipart form 'audio'=utterance.wav. client.ts:113.
apiCalls.responseHandling: Returns {text, status}. If text → sendMessage(text). status 'NoMatch' → '没听清楚,再说一次?'. PYTHON: record mic to 16kHz mono WAV with VAD, POST here, feed text into sendMessage equivalent.

apiCalls.when: Scripted branches classify the child's free answer (yesno/mood/activity-intent/sound-match/quit-activity/game-name/weather-mood/goodbye/etc). Not embodiment per se but the voice-text feeds it.
apiCalls.request: classifyIntent(text, schema, context?). client.ts:47.
apiCalls.responseHandling: Returns {label}; drives scripted branching. PYTHON: same — the transcribed/button-derived text goes here.

apiCalls.when: Every real (non-silent, non-override) user turn at top of sendMessage. Two-layer safety.
apiCalls.request: assessUserRisk(text, lastRobotLine). client.ts:71.
apiCalls.responseHandling: {emotion, risk_level}. high_risk→block+crisis line+end; concerning→concerningMode LLM turn; safe→emotion drives setFaceExpr. Fails safe to {neutral,safe}.

apiCalls.when: Playing any audio FILE (game sound effects, activity playlist tracks, comfort music). resolveSoundUrl HEAD-probes 6 extensions.
apiCalls.request: GET /api/audio/file/<encodeURIComponent(filename)>; resolveSoundUrl tries .mp3/.m4a/.wav/.ogg/.aac/.flac via HEAD until 200.
apiCalls.responseHandling: URL handed to new Audio(url). PYTHON: fetch/stream these files to the speaker; do extension probing the same way OR resolve once at bundle-load.

apiCalls.when: Continuous Voice Live mode only (alternative duplex path).
apiCalls.request: VoiceLiveClient.connect; streams {type:'input_audio_buffer.append', audio:base64 24kHz PCM16}; commits on stop.
apiCalls.responseHandling: Server relays Azure events: response.audio.delta (play), response.animation_viseme.delta (mouth), xi.expression (face), transcripts. PYTHON: optional — robot may prefer the simpler /api/transcribe path.

apiCalls.when: Session start (handleStartChatting refetches config: games, conversationFlow, safety). fetchConfig/fetchSystemPrompt.
apiCalls.request: fetchConfig('default').
apiCalls.responseHandling: Populates gamesConfigRef, convFlowRef, safetyRef, default voice. PYTHON: load from the published StudioBundle (bundle.py) instead of live config.

maps.audioBehavior:
Three separate, simultaneously-capable HTMLAudio channels the Python layer must mirror as independent players:

(1) VOICE/SFX channel = `audioRef`. Holds EITHER the current TTS utterance (data:audio/mpeg base64 from /api/tts/visemes) OR a one-shot game sound effect (file URL). Mutually exclusive — `stopAudio()` pauses+nulls it and cancels the viseme rAF before any new utterance. `playSoundEffect(src)` awaits 'ended' so scripted game flow blocks on it. callTts with {waitForEnd:true} awaits playbackEnded so the next scripted line doesn't overlap.

(2) ACTIVITY BACKGROUND-MUSIC channel = `activityAudioRef`, driven by `activityPlaylist` state {playlist[], index, paused, playCount}. A useEffect creates a fresh new Audio per (playlist,index,playCount) change. Behaviors: co-creation plays each track playCount times then fades (1500ms rAF volume ramp) on the final pass and on 'ended' sends silent '继续'; scripted body-rhythm/breathing loop the playlist (index = (index+1)%len) as a backdrop for the whole activity; emotion-music-mapping advances on a separate 20s/section rAF timer (startEmotionMappingTimer) with a 3000ms fade, NOT on 'ended'. Error/watchdog (90s) force-advance so co-creation never stalls. pause/resume via a separate useEffect on activityPlaylist.paused.

(3) COMFORT-MUSIC channel = `comfortMusicAudioRef`, volume 0.7, single hidden element driven by `comfortMusic` state {filename,paused}; auto-clears on 'ended'/error; killed on session end or next user message.

Volume fades everywhere are done by mutating audio.volume inside requestAnimationFrame loops (no WebAudio gain for files). Selection is via /api/audio/file/:filename, with resolveSoundUrl HEAD-probing 6 extensions. PYTHON must reproduce: at least a voice channel + a background-music channel + a comfort channel, each with independent play/pause/volume-fade, plus the playCount/loop/timer-advance semantics per activity type. (The Voice Live duplex path uses a 4th, WebAudio gapless 24kHz PCM16 scheduler — VoiceLivePlayback — only relevant if the robot adopts Voice Live.)
maps.faceBehavior:
The face is driven by a SINGLE imperative state variable `faceExpr: ExpressionId` (one of the 16), set via `setFaceExpr(id)` from ~60 call sites — NOT by a scheduled expression timeline in this panel. Even though the server streams an expression timeline (SSE 'expression' event / Voice Live xi.expression), TestChat collapses it: it reduces the events to the single highest-confidence one and calls setFaceExpr(best.expressionId). The Face.tsx demo panel is the only place expressionTimeline is actually fed to the renderer.

Drive sources, in order they occur per turn: (a) sendMessage entry → 'listening'; (b) risk-assess emotion → mapped expression (happy/excited/calm/curious/confused/sad/anxious, map at TestChat.tsx:1422); (c) startChatStream dispatched → 'thinking'; (d) SSE expression events during stream → best-confidence expr; (e) just before speaking a line → a warm pose ('gentle'/'excited'/'happy'); (f) after TTS 'ended' → setFaceExpr('calm') on a 500–2000ms setTimeout; (g) errors → 'confused'/'anxious'.

The MOUTH is a separate override layer. When visemePlaybackMs >= 0 and visemeStream is non-empty, SVG2DRenderer (renderer rAF loop) finds the last viseme whose audioOffsetMs <= visemePlaybackMs, maps it via getMouthForViseme(visemeId) (visemeMap.ts: 22 IDs → {width,curve,open,round}), and blends it over the expression's mouth (lerp width 0.7, curve 0.5, open=viseme.open). While a viseme is active, idle (blink/breath/drift) is suppressed. Expression still drives EYES, color, glow, head-tilt; viseme only drives the mouth. Color encodes emotion family (per-expression color). Transitions between expressions are 300ms cubic-bezier tweens (no teleporting).

PYTHON face/renderer.py LCD equivalent must: (1) hold one current expression and tween eyes/color/tilt over ~300ms; (2) override mouth from the viseme stream indexed by the audio playback clock (audio.currentTime in the web port → the Pi audio player's position); (3) suppress idle while a viseme is active and resume ~500ms after the last viseme; (4) accept setFaceExpr-style imperative updates from the orchestration. Viseme lip-sync requires calling /api/tts/visemes and aligning the returned visemes[].audioOffsetMs against the speaker's playback position frame-by-frame — this is the single most important embodiment detail to port.
maps.buttonRelevance:
The web panel has a text box, a Send button, a mic (one-shot voice) button, a Voice Live toggle + spacebar PTT, mute, plus inline activity/playlist controls. On the robot (4 GPIO buttons + mic, NO keyboard/text box) these map as follows:

- key1 START/STOP → handleStartChatting (begins scripted intro: speaks getFirstMeetingQuestion via callTts) / handleEndSession (cancels stream, stops all 3 audio channels, comfort music, ends activity, setFaceExpr('calm')). These are already button-shaped — no text needed.
- key2 push-to-TALK → replaces BOTH the text box AND the mic button. Press = start mic capture (one-shot VoiceInputRecorder OR Voice Live startRecording), release/VAD = stop → transcribeAudio → text → sendMessage(text). The entire app funnels every input through sendMessage(text)/sendMessageRef.current(text); the text box is just one producer of that string. The robot's button+mic becomes another producer. The spacebar PTT logic (handlePttStart/handlePttStop) is the closest existing analog and maps directly to key2 down/up.
- key3 NEXT → maps to the silent advance the code already issues as sendMessageRef.current('继续',{silent:true}) (used by auto-advance timers and activity sections). A NEXT button = manual '继续'. Also maps to activity playlist next / skip.
- key4 REPEAT → re-speak the last assistant line: call callTts(lastAssistantContent) again (re-fetch visemes + replay). No web equivalent button exists; the robot adds it by re-invoking callTts on the last apiHistory assistant entry.

CRITICAL replacements for the Python port: every place that reads `input` / sets `setInput('')` / handles Enter/Send is a TEXT-BOX assumption that must be replaced by button+voice producing the same `text` argument to sendMessage. The classify/risk/scripted-branch machinery downstream is input-source-agnostic — it only sees the resolved text string. Mute toggle (mutedRef) gates callTts; the robot may hardwire unmuted. The numeric age answer (free-text '三岁了'/'5') currently comes from the text box → must come from voice transcription on the robot.
openQuestions: Will the robot use the simple one-shot /api/transcribe path (VoiceInputRecorder VAD → WAV → POST) or the Voice Live WS duplex path? They drive the face differently: one-shot uses the pre-fetched /api/tts/visemes timeline (audio-clock indexed); Voice Live uses realtime response.animation_viseme.delta + onAudioFrame playbackMs. Recommend one-shot for v1 to reuse callTts lip-sync exactly.

Lip-sync clock source on the Pi: the web port indexes visemes by HTMLAudioElement.currentTime each rAF frame.
