The Python audio player (pygame/sounddevice/ffplay) must expose an accurate playback position; if it can't, the port needs a wall-clock-from-play-start estimate aligned to visemes[].audioOffsetMs.

TestChat collapses the server's streamed expression TIMELINE into a single best-confidence setFaceExpr and never schedules sub-utterance expression changes. Should the robot honor the full timeline (schedule expression cues against audioOffsetMs like SVG2DRenderer supports but the panel doesn't use) for a livelier face, or replicate the panel's single-expression behavior exactly? The prompt says reproduce exactly — so default to single faceExpr.

mp3 decoding on the Pi: /api/tts/visemes returns base64 MP3. Confirm the Pi audio stack can decode MP3 (it can via ffmpeg/mpg123) and that latency from fetch→decode→play is acceptable for button responsiveness.

Three+ simultaneous audio channels (voice, activity bg-music, comfort) require a Pi mixer that can play and independently volume-fade multiple streams at once. Confirm the chosen Python audio lib supports concurrent mixed playback with per-stream volume ramps (the web port relies on the browser mixing multiple HTMLAudio elements).
REPEAT (key4) has no web analog — confirm the desired behavior is re-running callTts on the last assistant line (re-fetching visemes) vs caching the last audio+viseme blob for instant replay.
Volume fades are rAF-driven (60fps) in the browser; the Pi should use a timer thread at a sane rate (e.g. 30–60 Hz) — confirm acceptable.

The watchdog/auto-advance timers (90s fallback, 20s emotion windows, co-creation playCount loops) are tightly coupled to audio 'ended' events; the Python audio layer must emit reliable end-of-track callbacks for these to work, otherwise activities stall.

maps.area: Server HTTP/WS API contract (Python runtime client spec)
maps.summary:
Maps every server route the Pi Python runtime calls. Base URL on the robot is http://127.0.0.1:8787 (server binds 127.0.0.1:PORT, PORT from .env, default 8787). On the studio these go through a Vite proxy /api → :8787; on the robot call :8787 directly, no proxy. CORS only allows localhost:5173/5174 origins (irrelevant for a same-host Python client — CORS is browser-only). Auth is a stub: every request gets req.user={id:'local-dev'}, no token/header needed.

THE NINE ENDPOINTS the runtime needs:
1. POST /api/chat — SSE streaming brain. This is the core. Body {configId,childAge,messages[],activityContext?,concerningMode?}. Streams `data: {...}\n\n` events of type text|expression|tool_call|done|error. Drives face + audio + activity orchestration. Tool calls (start_activity, play_melody, end_activity) come back INLINE as tool_call events carrying audioPlaylist / filename / playCount — the runtime plays those audio files. The server ALSO emits the scripted narration text itself (as text events) right after a tool_call, so the runtime speaks server text verbatim via TTS.
2. POST /api/tts — text → MP3 audio stream (audio/mpeg, 24kHz 48kbit mono). Runtime pipes to speaker. No visemes.
3. POST /api/tts/visemes — text → JSON {audio:base64-mp3, visemes:[{audioOffsetMs,visemeId}], ssml}. Use when you want lip-sync on the LCD face.
4. POST /api/classify — single-label intent classifier (10 schemas: yesno, mood, goodbye, activity-intent, task-completed, sound-match, quit-activity, weather-mood, game-name, assistant-distress). Returns {label}. Replaces text-box parsing of the child's voice transcript in the scripted flow / games.
5. POST /api/risk-assess — front-line safety classifier, run on EVERY child turn before chat. Returns {emotion, risk_level: safe|concerning|high_risk}. Drives distress branch + face emotion.
6. POST /api/transcribe — multipart WAV (16-bit mono 16kHz) → {text, status}. THE bridge from mic audio to text on the robot (no text box exists). status NoMatch ⇒ re-prompt.
7. GET /api/config/:id — load StudioConfig (activities, personas, voice, expression library). Runtime loads at boot. (PUT exists but robot is read-only.)
8. GET /api/audio + GET /api/audio/file/:filename — list / stream audio files (Range supported). Runtime resolves the audioPlaylist/filename strings from chat tool_calls against this.
9. GET /api/publish/active + GET /api/publish — which published StudioBundle version is active (robot loads this bundle). GET /api/system-prompt?configId&childAge — debug only, runtime does not need it.

Voice Live (GET /api/voice-live WS) is a browser-only realtime path (PCM16 24kHz, AudioWorklet). The robot's R3 flow uses the REST chat+TTS+transcribe path, NOT voice-live, so it is documented as a non-required alternative.

TEXT-BOX REPLACEMENT NOTE: the studio feeds typed text into /api/chat messages[], /api/classify text, /api/risk-assess text. On the robot every one of those `text` strings must come from /api/transcribe of the key2 push-to-talk mic capture (or a button mapping for fixed choices). No endpoint itself assumes a text box — the text box only lives in TestChat.tsx; the API is input-source agnostic.

states.name: SSE: text event
states.purpose: Incremental assistant speech tokens. data: {type:'text', delta:string}
states.enteredWhen: Model streams content, OR server emits scripted/canonical narration directly (section text, play_melody speakText, co-creation closing).
states.onEnter: Runtime appends delta to current utterance buffer. Accumulate deltas; when an utterance/turn is complete, send the assembled text to POST /api/tts (or /api/tts/visemes) and play. Note: server may emit a whole scripted section as one or few large text deltas right after a tool_call — treat identically.

transitions.sideEffects: keep buffering
none
none
finalize utterance, run TTS on full text if not already streamed

states.name: SSE: expression event
states.purpose: Face expression timeline from clause-sentiment classifier. data: {type:'expression', timeline: ExpressionEvent[]} where ExpressionEvent={atCharOffset:number, expressionId:string, confidence:number}.
states.enteredWhen: Classifier detects a clause-level sentiment shift in streamed text (server calls classifier.feed per delta and classifier.flush at end).
states.onEnter: Runtime schedules face expression changes: expressionId is one of the 16 expressions; atCharOffset is the character index in the assistant text at which to switch. On the Pi, align atCharOffset to the corresponding TTS audio offset (or just apply on speech of that clause). Drive face/renderer.py setExpression(expressionId).

transitions.sideEffects: none

states.name: SSE: tool_call event
states.purpose: Activity orchestration signal. data: {type:'tool_call', name:string, args:object, result:ToolCallResult}. name ∈ start_activity | play_melody | end_activity.
states.enteredWhen: Model (or server-forced tool_choice) invokes a tool; server resolves it and emits the resolved result inline, then continues streaming.
states.onEnter: Runtime branches on name. start_activity result: {ok, activityId, activityName, activityType, audioPlaylist:string[], currentSectionText?, sectionNumber?, totalSections?, interactive?, matchedBucket?} — set active-activity state, play audioPlaylist files (resolve via /api/audio/file/:filename), the server will ALSO emit currentSectionText as a following text event to speak. play_melody result: {ok, notes, variant:'original'|'revised'|'background', filename, playCount, speakText} — play the file `filename` playCount times (background loops 2× then auto-advance); speak speakText (also emitted as text). end_activity result: {ok, speakText?} — stop looping audio, clear activity badge/state, speak speakText (co-creation closing). On result.error: speak/handle the error gently, do not crash.

transitions.sideEffects: speak it via TTS
none

states.name: SSE: done event
states.purpose: Turn complete. data: {type:'done', usage:{promptTokens, completionTokens}}
states.enteredWhen: Generation finished (no more tool calls, model stopped).
states.onEnter: Runtime closes the HTTP stream read loop, ensures full utterance has been spoken via TTS, returns control to the orchestration FSM (await next button/voice). usage is informational only.

transitions.sideEffects: resume idle face daydream

states.name: SSE: error event
states.purpose: Stream failure. data: {type:'error', message:string}
states.enteredWhen: Upstream Azure stream dropped after a retry, content-filter block, or other unrecoverable error. Server already auto-retries ONCE on a transient drop if nothing was emitted yet.
states.onEnter: Runtime should speak a gentle fallback line and/or set a sad/gentle face; message text for transient drops literally says 'please send the message again' — runtime can auto-resend the same turn once. Do NOT display raw message to the child.

transitions.sideEffects: optionally auto-retry the same /api/chat request once

states.name: SSE wire format (parsing rule)
states.purpose: How to parse the chat stream in Python.
states.enteredWhen: Reading the /api/chat response body.

transitions.sideEffects: route to text/expression/tool_call/done/error handler

apiCalls.when: The main brain call. Robot calls it for: the scripted intro turns, every conversational child reply, starting/running the 4 activities, and the co-creation game. Call after transcribing the child's push-to-talk (key2) speech and after /api/risk-assess clears the turn.
apiCalls.request: JSON. {configId?:string (default 'default'), childAge:int 1-120 (REQUIRED), messages:[{role:'user'|'assistant', content:string}] (min 1, full running history), activityContext?:{activityId?,activityName?,type?:'breathing'|'body-rhythm'|'emotion-music-mapping'|'co-creation',description?,therapyMode?:bool,sectionIndex?:int>=0,coCreationLastVariant?:'none'|'original'|'revised'|'background',coCreationNotes?:string[]}, concerningMode?:bool}. Set concerningMode=true when /api/risk-assess returned 'concerning' (server then forbids start_activity and lowers temperature). Set activityContext.sectionIndex to advance scripted activity sections turn-by-turn. For co-creation set coCreationLastVariant + coCreationNotes so the server forces the right tool. Validation 400 on bad body; 404 if config missing.
apiCalls.responseHandling: SSE stream (see states). Dispatch text→TTS+speak, expression→face, tool_call→activity/audio orchestration, done→end turn, error→gentle fallback. IMPORTANT: server emits scripted narration as text events itself — speak server text verbatim; do not also synthesize the model's pre-tool lead-in (server suppresses it). Tool result audioPlaylist/filename are bare filenames to resolve via /api/audio/file/:filename. play_melody background variant: play file playCount(2)× then advance to closing. Status 200 always (errors come as in-band error events); a non-200 means request was rejected (400/404).

apiCalls.when: Whenever the robot needs to speak any text aloud: assistant chat text, scripted lines, prompts. The primary speech-output call.
apiCalls.request: JSON {text:string 1-3000 (REQUIRED), voice?:string (default zh-CN-XiaoxiaoMultilingualNeural), style?:string (default 'cheerful' — per-activity SSML style), rate?:string, pitch?:string}. Server runs ttsSanitizer (strips quotes/markdown/emoji, wraps digits) and builds SSML.
apiCalls.responseHandling: Returns a streamed audio/mpeg body: MP3 audio-24khz-48kbitrate-mono. Pipe bytes straight to the Pi audio player (e.g. write to mpg123/ffplay stdin or a temp file). 400 on invalid body, 502 on Azure/TTS failure (JSON {error,...}). No viseme/timing data on this route.

apiCalls.when: When the robot wants lip-synced face animation on the LCD while speaking (mouth shapes driven by visemes). Optional alternative to /api/tts.
apiCalls.request: JSON same shape as /api/tts {text,voice?,style?,rate?,pitch?}. Uses the Speech SDK.
apiCalls.responseHandling: Returns JSON {audio:base64 MP3 (24kHz 48kbit mono), visemes:[{audioOffsetMs:int, visemeId:int}] sorted by offset, ssml:string}. Decode base64 audio → play; schedule face/visemeMap.py mouth shapes at each audioOffsetMs. 400 invalid body, 502 synth failure.

apiCalls.when: Inside scripted intro + games, to interpret the child's transcribed voice (or a button choice) into a fixed label WITHOUT a hardcoded keyword list. Replaces text-box answer parsing. Called e.g. after transcribing a yes/no, a mood, a weather word, a game name, a sound-detective guess, 'did you finish?', 'do you want to quit?'.
apiCalls.request: JSON {text:string 1-500 (REQUIRED, the child's transcript), schema:enum (REQUIRED) one of 'yesno'|'mood'|'goodbye'|'activity-intent'|'task-completed'|'sound-match'|'quit-activity'|'weather-mood'|'game-name'|'assistant-distress', context?:string<=500 (e.g. expected answer for sound-match — appended as 'Expected: ...')}.
apiCalls.responseHandling: Returns {label:string}. Allowed labels per schema: yesno→yes|no|unclear; mood→positive|negative|neutral|unclear; goodbye→yes|no; activity-intent→yes|no; task-completed→yes|no; sound-match→yes|no; quit-activity→yes|no; weather-mood→sunny|cloudy|rainy|snowy|thunder|unclear; game-name→rhythm|co-creation|breathing|emotion-mapping|unclear; assistant-distress→yes|no. Fails open: on model error returns the LAST label (the 'unclear'/'no' default) — runtime should re-prompt on that. temperature 0, max_tokens 4. 400 on invalid body.

apiCalls.when: FIRST thing on every child turn, before /api/chat. Layer-1 safety gate. Run on the transcribed child utterance.
apiCalls.request: JSON {text:string 1-2000 (REQUIRED), context?:string<=1000 (what the robot just said, to disambiguate vague replies — never lowers a self-harm/death/violence signal)}.
apiCalls.responseHandling: Returns {emotion: one of happy|sad|angry|scared|anxious|excited|calm|confused|curious|neutral, risk_level: safe|concerning|high_risk}. Dispatch: high_risk → BLOCK the turn, do NOT call /api/chat normally; escalate (route child to a trusted adult/nurse), end/pause session per safety flow. concerning → call /api/chat with concerningMode=true. safe → normal flow. Use emotion to drive the face without a 2nd model call. Fails open to {neutral,safe} on network/auth errors; fails CLOSED (high_risk) on Azure content-filter self_harm/violence blocks. Always 200 (or 400 invalid body).

apiCalls.when: After capturing key2 push-to-talk mic audio. THE only way to turn the child's voice into text on the robot (no keyboard). Output text feeds /api/risk-assess, /api/classify, and /api/chat messages[].
apiCalls.request: multipart/form-data, single field 'audio' = WAV (16-bit mono 16kHz PCM). Optional query ?language=zh-CN (default zh-CN). 5MB upload limit. Client sends FormData with filename like utterance.wav.
apiCalls.responseHandling: Returns {text:string, status:string}. status RecognitionStatus: 'Success'|'NoMatch'|'InitialSilenceTimeout'|'BabbleTimeout'|'Error'. Empty text + 'NoMatch'/silence ⇒ speak a gentle re-prompt and re-capture. 400 if no audio field, 502 on Azure STT error, 503 if AZURE_SPEECH_KEY unset, 500 other. Trim the returned text before use.

apiCalls.when: At robot boot, to load the active StudioConfig (id usually 'default'): activities[], personas[], voice settings, expression library, age routing, safety. Needed to resolve voice/style for TTS and activity metadata.
apiCalls.request: No body. Path param :id (e.g. 'default').
apiCalls.responseHandling: Returns the full StudioConfig JSON. 404 {error} if not found. (PUT /api/config/:id replaces config and stamps updatedAt — robot is read-only, does not call PUT.)

apiCalls.when: To resolve and play the audio referenced by chat tool_call results (start_activity.audioPlaylist[], play_melody.filename) and any config-referenced clips. List once at boot to cache available files; stream each file when an activity needs it.
apiCalls.request: GET /api/audio: no body → returns AudioFileEntry[] {filename,sizeBytes,mimeType,modifiedMs} sorted by name. GET /api/audio/file/:filename: path param is a bare filename (path.basename validated; traversal rejected with 400). HTTP Range header supported for seeking/streaming.
apiCalls.responseHandling: File route returns the audio bytes with proper Content-Type (audio/mpeg etc.), 206 on Range, 416 on bad range, 404 if missing/not-an-audio-ext (.mp3/.wav/.m4a/.ogg/.aac/.flac). Pipe to Pi audio player. The runtime maps tool_call playlist strings 1:1 to /api/audio/file/<string>.

apiCalls.when: At boot / on config-refresh, to learn which published StudioBundle version is active (the robot consumes the published bundle, per CLAUDE.md §12 future endpoint). Optional for R3 if the robot reads configs/ directly, but this is the canonical 'what should I run' pointer.
apiCalls.request: No body.
apiCalls.responseHandling: GET /api/publish/active → active pointer object. GET /api/publish → {active, versions[]}. (POST /api/publish and POST /api/publish/rollback are studio-authoring actions; the robot does not call them.)

apiCalls.when: Runtime startup probe / readiness check before driving the experience — confirm the Node brain is up on :8787.
apiCalls.request: No body.
apiCalls.responseHandling: Returns {ok:true, ts:ISOstring}. Use to gate the FSM until the brain is reachable.

apiCalls.when: NOT required for R3. Browser-only realtime voice path (Voice Live). The robot's button-driven flow uses REST chat+tts+transcribe instead. Documented as a future full-duplex alternative.
apiCalls.request: WS upgrade with query configId, childAge (1-120 required). After connect, server proxies to Azure Voice Live; expects PCM16 24kHz mono audio frames and realtime-API JSON events from the client.
apiCalls.responseHandling: Server sends {type:'xi.ready'} when session initialized, {type:'xi.expression',events:[ExpressionEvent]} for face timeline, {type:'xi.error',message} on error, and forwards all Azure realtime events (audio deltas, transcripts). Would require AudioWorklet-equivalent PCM streaming on the Pi; out of scope for the button/REST runtime.

maps.audioBehavior: Audio comes from two server sources the runtime must merge. (1) TTS speech: send assistant/scripted text to POST /api/tts → MP3 stream → play on the Pi speaker. Resolve voice+style from the StudioConfig / per-activity SSML style. (2) Pre-recorded activity audio: chat tool_call results carry bare filenames — start_activity.audioPlaylist[] (sequence to play through), play_melody.filename (single clip, play playCount times; 'background' variant = playCount 2 then auto-advance to the closing turn; 'original'/'revised' = once). Resolve each filename via GET /api/audio/file/:filename (Range supported) and play. end_activity stops any looping/background track. The server interleaves: it emits a tool_call (telling you which audio to play) then text events (the narration to TTS-speak) — the runtime sequences narration TTS and playlist audio per the scripted flow. No audio is embedded in chat; everything is a filename or a TTS text string.
maps.faceBehavior: Face is driven by SSE expression events from /api/chat (and xi.expression from voice-live if ever used). Each expression event is {type:'expression', timeline:[{atCharOffset, expressionId, confidence}]} where expressionId is one of the 16 expressions and atCharOffset is the char index into the assistant text where the expression should change. The runtime schedules face/renderer.py setExpression at the audio offset corresponding to that char (or applies per-clause as that text is spoken). Additionally /api/risk-assess returns an `emotion` label (happy/sad/angry/scared/anxious/excited/calm/confused/curious/neutral) the runtime can map to a face the instant the child speaks, before chat responds, with no extra model call. /api/tts/visemes additionally yields per-offset visemeId for mouth-shape lip-sync (face/visemeMap.py). Idle daydream resumes after the done event when no speech is active.
maps.buttonRelevance: No endpoint assumes a text box — the box exists only in TestChat.tsx. Mapping to the 4 buttons + voice: key2 (push-to-TALK) captures mic audio → POST /api/transcribe → the resulting text is what gets fed to /api/risk-assess, /api/classify, and /api/chat messages[] (replacing every place TestChat read the typed input). key1 (START/STOP) gates the session: START triggers the boot probe (/api/health), config load (/api/config/:id, /api/publish/active) and the first scripted /api/chat turn; STOP ends the session (can map to the goodbye flow). key3 (NEXT) advances scripted activity sections — implemented by re-calling /api/chat with activityContext.sectionIndex incremented (and silent 'continue' advances in co-creation). key4 (REPEAT) re-plays the last TTS/audio (re-call /api/tts with the same text, or replay the cached MP3) — no server state needed. Fixed-choice prompts (yes/no, weather, game pick) can be answered EITHER by voice (transcribe → /api/classify) OR by mapping buttons to canonical labels, bypassing transcription. The classify schemas (weather-mood, game-name, yesno, etc.) are exactly the fixed choice sets a button menu would offer.
openQuestions: Does the Pi runtime read configs/ and audio/ off the local disk directly (server runs on the same Pi, DATA_DIR local), or strictly via the HTTP audio/config routes? If direct-disk, /api/audio/file and /api/config become optional, but tool_call filenames still need a resolution path — confirm which the Python port uses.

activityContext is studio-managed state (sectionIndex, coCreationLastVariant, coCreationNotes) that the server trusts/patches. The Python runtime must own this state machine itself (the server does not persist it between requests). Confirm the runtime will track section index and co-creation stage exactly as TestChat does — the section-advance and co-creation forced-tool logic depend on it being correct.

The server emits scripted narration text itself AND suppresses the model's pre-tool lead-in — so on the robot we must NOT additionally TTS the model's discarded text. Need to confirm the Python SSE consumer speaks ONLY the text events received (which already include server-authored narration), never re-deriving narration from tool_call.currentSectionText/speakText (those are duplicated as text events).
Whether R3 uses /api/tts (no visemes, simpler, streams) or /api/tts/visemes (lip-sync, one buffered JSON blob, higher latency) for the LCD face — affects whether mouth animation is wired.

high_risk handling: the API only classifies — it does not perform escalation. The Python runtime must implement the actual high_risk branch (block chat, route to adult, end/pause). Confirm what 'escalate to caregiver' means on a robot with no screen and no monitoring (cut from v1): likely a spoken safety line + sad/gentle face + nurse-call, to be defined.

voice-live WS is browser/AudioWorklet-shaped (PCM16 24kHz). Confirmed out of scope for the button/REST runtime — but if low-latency full-duplex voice is later wanted on the Pi, the WS protocol (xi.ready/xi.expression/xi.error + Azure realtime passthrough) is the path; flag whether to build a Python PCM client for it.

design.rationale: The maps make the split unambiguous. The orchestration that must move to the robot is ~95% UI-state choreography that is meaningless on the server, and the heavy lifting is already pure server primitives the Pi can call over HTTP. Concretely: (1) The scripted intro + 2 warmup games + safety gate + recommended-game funnel are a deterministic client FSM keyed on one string (scriptedSessionStepRef / ScriptedSessionStep) plus a handful of refs (childAgeRef, game2SoundRef, gamePool, introducedGames, comfortMusicCursor, userTurnCount, breakDue). Every transition is `classify(transcript) -> label -> speak a fixed conversationFlow constant -> set next step`. None of that touches Azure directly — it only sequences calls to /api/classify, /api/risk-assess, /api/tts, /api/audio/file. That is exactly what a thin Python FSM is good at, and net/brain.py already implements the client. (2) The genuinely server-side intelligence — LLM chat with tool calls (start_activity / play_melody / end_activity), clause-sentiment expression timeline, the risk classifier prompt with content-filter fail-closed, the 10 classify schemas, TTS+SSML sanitizer, STT — is ALREADY in apps/server and verified working on the Pi at 127.0.0.1:8787. The 4 activities run server-side via the tool_call protocol; the runtime is a dumb sequencer of text/expression/tool_call/done events. So there is almost nothing left to 'extract' — the server already owns the brain. (3) extract-fsm-to-server-node is actively wrong here because the FSM's transitions are driven by EMBODIMENT events the server cannot see: button edges (key1/2/3/4), audio 'ended' callbacks (the waitForEnd sequencing, playCount loops, emotion-mapping 20s timers, 90s watchdog), viseme playback-clock position, mic VAD endpointing, and mute state. A server FSM would need a bidirectional event channel back to the Pi for every press and every track-end — reinventing a worse Voice-Live. The TestChat evidence is decisive: the FSM lives next to the audio elements and button handlers, not next to the network. A hybrid is unnecessary: there is no third slice that wants to be on the server. The one nuance — activityContext state (sectionIndex, coCreationLastVariant, coCreationNotes) — is owned by the CLIENT today (the server does not persist it between /api/chat calls), so the Python runtime must own it too; that reinforces python-reimplement-fsm rather than splitting it.
design.fsmOverview: One top-level session FSM in the Python embodiment (robot/session/fsm.py), a faithful port of TestChat's scriptedSessionStep machine plus the LLM-handoff (step==='none') and safety pre-emption. States: BOOT -> IDLE -> (key1) INTRO_FIRST_MEETING -> {INTRO_AGE | INTRO_RETURNING_AGE} -> ... -> WARMUP (game-1-completion | game-2-answer) -> WEATHER_CHOICE -> GAME_PICK -> GAME_DECIDE -> LLM (step 'none', free chat + activities) -> GOODBYE -> IDLE. Crosscutting: a SAFETY_GATE wraps EVERY real user turn before the step machine (high_risk -> BLOCKED_CRISIS -> end; concerning -> CONCERNING_RESPONSE -> CONCERNING_MUSIC_OFFER, session stays alive). An ACTIVITY sub-FSM (driven entirely by /api/chat tool_call + text events, with the runtime owning activityContext sectionIndex / co-creation variant state) runs while step==='none' and a start_activity has fired. The FSM holds exactly the refs TestChat holds: current_step:str, child_age:int, sounds/game2_sound, game_pool:list, introduced_games:set, comfort_music_cursor:int (persistent), user_turn_count:int, break_due:bool, last_assistant_line:str (for REPEAT), muted:bool, plus activity state. The crucial inversion vs the web app: TestChat is event-driven by React; the robot is a single main loop that (a) renders the face every frame, (b) polls buttons, (c) on a finalized utterance string runs the same turn pipeline: pauseComfortMusic -> risk-assess -> [keyword filter] -> dispatch (BLOCKED/CONCERNING/scripted-step/LLM). dispatchScriptedOrBypass (the activity-intent bypass — a hard project rule) wraps the scripted steps exactly as in TestChat. The intro never calls /api/chat; only /api/classify, /api/risk-assess, /api/tts(/visemes), /api/audio/file. The LLM only enters at step 'none'.

states.name: BOOT
states.purpose: Bring the runtime up and confirm the Node brain is reachable before any child interaction.
transitions: -> IDLE once /api/health returns ok and config+bundle loaded
states.sideEffects: Loop on brain.health() until ok (gate the FSM). Load active StudioBundle via bundle.load_active (audioManifest, config.face.expressionLibrary colors, conversationFlow, games, safety). Cache get_config('default') into convFlow/games/safety refs. Start the face render thread at 'calm' with idle daydream. No audio.

states.name: IDLE
states.purpose: Session-idle. Face daydreams; wait for key1=START.
states.sideEffects: setFaceExpr('calm'); idle loop (blink/breath/drift) active. All 3 audio channels silent. comfort_music_cursor persists. On entry from a prior session: reset current_step='none', child_age=default, clear transcript/apiHistory/game refs, user_turn_count=0, break_due=false.

states.name: INTRO_FIRST_MEETING
states.purpose: Port of handleStartChatting + 'first-meeting'. Ask if first meeting; branch new vs returning.
states.sideEffects: On entry (key1): refresh config, clear state, sessionActive=true. Speak getFirstMeetingQuestion() via callTts; face gentle->calm 500ms after. Each child reply: face 'thinking' while classifying. yes -> speak getStartChattingIntro() (asks age). no -> speak OLD_FRIEND_RECOGNITION + getAgePrompt(). NOTE goodbyePromise forced false while step!='none' so an ASR'd '是/嗯/对' can't end the session mid-intro.

states.name: INTRO_AGE
states.purpose: New-friend age capture; deliver age-gated weather/mood prompt, then hand to LLM.
transitions: age parsed (parseAgeFromText) -> LLM (step 'none', weather answer goes to model)
states.sideEffects: parseAgeFromText(transcript) handles ASCII + Chinese numerals; if in [1,120] set child_age (else AGE_DEFAULT). Speak weatherPromptForAge(getWeatherPrompt(), age) — drops snow option for age<=7. step->'none' (matches plain 'age' path; weather answer is LLM-handled). face gentle->calm. (age-short variant -> WEATHER_CHOICE is reserved/unused on the linear path.)

states.name: INTRO_RETURNING_AGE
states.purpose: Returning-friend age capture, then daily-story intro.
states.sideEffects: Parse+store age. Speak OLD_FRIEND_AGE_TO_STORY + a random pickReturningIntroFromConfig() daily story (ends '你今天过得怎么样呢?'). step->'returning-intro-answer'. face gentle->calm.

states.name: INTRO_RETURNING_ANSWER
states.purpose: Mood-mirror reply to the daily story, then launch ONE coin-flip warmup game.
states.sideEffects: moodIntroAnswerResponse via classify('mood') -> one of 4 fixed mirror lines. Game2 branch: speak moodReply+getGame2Intro() (waitForEnd) -> playSoundEffect(sound.src from /api/audio/file) -> speak sound.question; set game2_sound; step->'game-2-answer'. Game1 branch: speak moodReply+getGame1Prefix()+random rhythm story; step->'game-1-completion'. face 'thinking'->'gentle'.

states.name: WARMUP_GAME1 (game-1-completion)
states.purpose: Rhythm-story: child claps then says/presses done; detect completion.
transitions: key3=NEXT press -> WEATHER_CHOICE (treat as definitive done; skips the lossy classifier)
classify('task-completed')=='no' -> LLM (abandon scripted funnel, re-send raw text)
states.sideEffects: face 'thinking' while classifying. yes -> speak random pickGame1Completion() + weatherPromptForAge(); step->'weather-game-choice'; face gentle->calm. no -> un-append turn, step->'none', re-send raw text to LLM. No prerecorded audio (pure TTS narration).

states.name: WARMUP_GAME2 (game-2-answer)
states.purpose: Sound-detective: grade the child's guess leniently, respond, then enter weather funnel.
transitions: key4=REPEAT press -> replay game2_sound clip, stay in state
states.sideEffects: isSoundAnswerCorrect = classify('sound-match', text, context=sound.label) -> correctResponse/wrongResponse. fixedReply = baseReply + weatherPromptForAge(). game2_sound=null after answering. face thinking->gentle->calm. Sound resolved from bundle audioManifest by exact audioFilename (no HEAD-probe needed on Pi).

states.name: WEATHER_CHOICE (weather-game-choice)
states.purpose: Child names a weather standing for mood; seed the recommended-game pool.
states.sideEffects: face 'thinking'. Speak SUNNY_RECOMMENDATION or quietRecommendationLocal(mirror). Reset introduced_games=set(), current_game=null. Pool entries are RECOMMENDED_GAMES ids = activity ALIASES, not real GameConfigs.

states.name: GAME_PICK (game-pick)
states.purpose: Child names one of the 4 recommended games; show its card, ask whether to start. NOT wrapped in activity-intent bypass (bare game names would falsely trip it).
transitions: unclear -> GAME_PICK (re-ask via secondary classify('yesno'): vague vs unmatched line)
states.sideEffects: face 'thinking'. On match: remove from pool, introduced_games.add, current_game=id, speak RECOMMENDED_GAMES[id].card + GAME_DECIDE_PROMPT.

states.name: GAME_DECIDE (game-decide)
states.purpose: Start the chosen activity, switch to a different card, or browse the next. NOT bypass-wrapped.
transitions: classify('yesno')=='yes' -> LLM+START_ACTIVITY (silent message forces start_activity tool)
named different valid game (yesno!='yes') -> GAME_DECIDE (re-route to that card)
states.sideEffects: Run classify('yesno') and classify('game-name') in PARALLEL; yes/no wins ties. yes -> speak GAME_START_LINE, face 'excited', step->'none', then sendMessage('开始'+displayName+'吧', silent=True) so the LLM calls start_activity. (Hand-off to ACTIVITY sub-FSM.)

states.name: GAME_POOL_EXHAUSTED
states.purpose: All recommended games declined; drop scaffolding to the LLM.
states.sideEffects: step->'none'; re-dispatch the child's text to /api/chat (non-silent). All subsequent turns are free-form LLM.

states.name: LLM (step 'none' free chat + ACTIVITY sub-FSM)
states.purpose: Free-form conversation and the 4 activities, driven by /api/chat SSE (text/expression/tool_call/done).
transitions: tool_call end_activity -> clear activity, back to free chat
classify('goodbye')=='yes' after TTS (only when step=='none') -> GOODBYE
key3=NEXT -> advance: sendMessage('继续', silent=True) or activityContext.sectionIndex+1
states.sideEffects: face 'thinking' on dispatch; speak ONLY server text events verbatim (server authors scripted narration and suppresses the model's pre-tool lead-in — do NOT re-TTS tool_call.speakText/currentSectionText). expression timeline -> setFaceExpr(best-confidence) (single faceExpr, not scheduled). onDone -> callTts(full text) then schedule auto-advance. Activity audio: start_activity.audioPlaylist[] and play_melody.filename via activityAudioRef channel with playCount/loop/20s-emotion-timer/90s-watchdog semantics; end_activity stops it. Runtime owns activityContext {sectionIndex, coCreationLastVariant, coCreationNotes}.

states.name: SAFETY_GATE (crosscutting, every real turn)
states.purpose: Two-layer distress screen run BEFORE the step machine on every non-silent, non-override utterance.
transitions: safe -> emotion->face via hardcoded switch, continue to step machine
states.sideEffects: On entry: cancelAutoAdvance; pauseComfortMusic (stop comfort channel). context = last assistant line sliced -300. assessUserRisk -> {emotion, risk_level}. Then local bidirectional-substring keyword match over safety.distressKeywords (only if not already high_risk). Fails SAFE on network error (still runs keyword filter). NOTE: net/brain.py currently posts /api/risk and reads a 'level' key — must be fixed to POST /api/risk-assess and read {emotion, risk_level}.

states.name: BLOCKED_CRISIS
states.purpose: Deterministic crisis redirect: speak fixed hotline line, then END session. Distress text never reaches the cloud LLM.
states.sideEffects: Tear down audio/visemes; endActivity if running. Append HIGH_RISK_RESPONSE (hotlines 12355 / 400-161-9995) to VISIBLE transcript ONLY (never apiHistory). face 'gentle'; callTts(HIGH_RISK_RESPONSE, waitForEnd=True) so end-session doesn't cut it; face 'calm' after 2000ms; handleEndSession(). On a screenless robot, also consider a distinct caregiver signal (LED/nurse-call) — open question.

states.name: CONCERNING_RESPONSE / CONCERNING_MUSIC_OFFER
states.purpose: Comfort reply for distress-not-crisis; offer soft music; session stays alive.
transitions: next turn classify('yesno')=='yes' -> play next comfort track + COMFORT_MUSIC_ACCEPTED -> LLM
states.sideEffects: Append CONCERNING_RESPONSE to VISIBLE transcript only (not apiHistory); face gentle->calm. step->'concerning-music-offer' (this step deliberately SKIPS the activity-intent bypass). On yes: comfort track = safety.comfortMusicFiles[comfort_music_cursor % len] (round-robin, persistent cursor, NOT random) on the comfort channel; advance cursor. step->'none' either way.

states.name: GOODBYE
states.purpose: Terminal teardown after crisis block or an explicit goodbye while step=='none'.
states.sideEffects: handleEndSession: cancel chat stream, stop all 3 audio channels, clear comfort music, endActivity, sessionActive=false, step='none', clear game/pool refs, user_turn_count=0, break_due=false, face 'calm'. comfort_music_cursor persists across sessions.

design.embodimentLoop: A single-threaded main loop in robot/main.py plus one background face-render thread, mirroring how TestChat funnels everything through sendMessage(text)/callTts(text)/setFaceExpr(id). FACE THREAD (~20 FPS, the run_face.py loop generalized): every frame compute state_with_idle(EXPRESSIONS-or-bundle.expression(current_expr), idle.update(now)), then if a viseme stream is active (viseme_playback_ms>=0) override the mouth from the viseme indexed by the audio player's playback position, then disp.show(render(...)). The face thread reads two shared atomics set by the main loop: current_expr (one ExpressionId, 300ms-tweened) and the active viseme timeline + playback clock. idle.suppress(now, dur) is called while speaking and resumes ~500ms after the last viseme — exactly the existing IdleBehavior contract. MAIN LOOP: (1) poll buttons via hw.buttons (poll() for momentary key1/key3/key4 edges, is_held(TALK) for push-to-talk). (2) key1 -> if IDLE handleStart (enter INTRO_FIRST_MEETING), else handleEndSession (-> GOODBYE/IDLE). (3) key2 held -> start mic capture (16kHz mono WAV, RMS VAD: speech>0.02, 800ms trailing silence ends, 4s no-speech cancel, 15s cap — the VoiceInputRecorder contract); on release/VAD-stop -> brain.transcribe(wav,'zh-CN') -> if text, feed it into the ONE turn pipeline run_turn(text); face 'listening' during capture, 'thinking' during transcribe. (4) key3=NEXT -> run_turn('继续', silent=True) (the silent auto-advance the code already issues) or, in WARMUP_GAME1, a hard 'done'; also activity section/playlist skip. (5) key4=REPEAT -> re-speak last_assistant_line via callTts (re-fetch visemes), or in WARMUP_GAME2 replay game2_sound. run_turn(text, silent, override) is the heart: if not silent and not override -> SAFETY_GATE (risk-assess + keyword); then dispatchScriptedOrBypass if current_step is a scripted step (activity-intent check first, then the step handler), else the LLM path (chat_stream consume -> speak text events, set face from expression events, handle tool_call activity audio, callTts on done). callTts(text, waitForEnd): brain.tts_visemes(text) -> decode base64 mp3 -> play on the VOICE channel -> set viseme timeline + clock so the face thread lip-syncs -> on 'ended' clear visemes, face->'calm' after 500ms, resume idle; if waitForEnd, block until 'ended' before returning (the load-bearing sequencing for safety lines, game intro->sound->question, end-session). AUDIO: three independent players (voice/sfx, activity-bg-music with playCount/loop/20s-timer/90s-watchdog, comfort-music round-robin) each with play/pause/volume-fade and a reliable on-ended callback (mpg123/ffplay/pygame.mixer with 3 channels) — the activity timers and co-creation playCount loops depend on accurate end-of-track callbacks. Every press and every audio-ended event is an FSM input; the FSM never blocks the face thread.
design.protocol: All calls hit the Node brain at http://127.0.0.1:8787 (stdlib urllib, no pip — net/brain.py already does this). No CORS, no auth (req.user is a local-dev stub). Endpoints the runtime uses, with exact shapes: (1) GET /api/health -> {ok,ts}; gate BOOT until ok. (2) GET /api/config/default -> full StudioConfig (voice/style, conversationFlow, games, safety, expressionLibrary). Loaded at boot; activity/voice metadata also resolvable from the published bundle via bundle.load_active (data/published/active.json -> vN.json). (3) POST /api/risk-assess {text, context?} -> {emotion: happy|sad|angry|scared|anxious|excited|calm|confused|curious|neutral, risk_level: safe|concerning|high_risk}. FIRST call on every real turn. context = robot's last line sliced to trailing 300 chars. Fails SAFE (neutral/safe) on network error; fails CLOSED (high_risk) on Azure self_harm/violence content-filter. BUG TO FIX: net/brain.py currently posts /api/risk and reads {level} — wrong endpoint and wrong key; change to /api/risk-assess and read risk_level + emotion. (4) POST /api/classify {text(1-500), schema, context?} -> {label}. 10 schemas: yesno(yes|no|unclear), mood(positive|negative|neutral|unclear), goodbye(yes|no), activity-intent(yes|no), task-completed(yes|no), sound-match(yes|no, context=expected sound label e.g. '鸡 Chicken'), quit-activity(yes|no), weather-mood(sunny|cloudy|rainy|snowy|thunder|unclear), game-name(rhythm|co-creation|breathing|emotion-mapping|unclear), assistant-distress(yes|no, DEAD/unused). temp 0, max_tokens 4, fails OPEN to the last label (treat as unclear/no -> re-prompt). This is the text-box replacement: every scripted branch is classify(transcript, schema). (5) POST /api/transcribe (multipart 'audio'=16-bit mono 16kHz WAV; ?language=zh-CN) -> {text, status}. status NoMatch/InitialSilenceTimeout -> gentle re-prompt + re-capture. THE only mic->text bridge (no keyboard). (6) POST /api/tts {text(1-3000),voice?,style?,rate?,pitch?} -> streamed audio/mpeg (MP3 24kHz 48kbit mono); server runs ttsSanitizer (quotes/markdown/emoji/digits) + builds SSML. Use for plain speech. (7) POST /api/tts/visemes {same body} -> {audio: base64 MP3, visemes:[{audioOffsetMs,visemeId}] sorted, ssml}. Use for LCD lip-sync: decode audio, play, index visemes by the player's playback position each face frame -> getMouthForViseme on the LCD. ADD this to net/brain.py (only /api/tts exists today). For R3 v1 default to /api/tts/visemes so the mouth moves; /api/tts is the fallback if buffered-JSON latency hurts button responsiveness. (8) GET /api/audio/file/:filename (bare filename, Range supported) -> audio bytes; resolves tool_call audioPlaylist[]/play_melody.filename and game2 sound/comfort tracks 1:1. On the Pi these can also be read straight off DATA_DIR/audio since the server is same-host — but the HTTP route is the canonical resolver. (9) POST /api/chat (SSE) {configId, childAge(REQUIRED 1-120), messages:[{role,content}], activityContext?, concerningMode?} -> stream of `data: {json}\n\n` events (NO event: line, NO [DONE] sentinel; terminal is {type:'done'}). Event types: text{delta} (buffer; speak via TTS — server emits scripted narration as text and SUPPRESSES the model's pre-tool lead-in, so speak ONLY received text events, never re-derive from tool_call fields), expression{timeline:[{atCharOffset,expressionId,confidence}]} (-> setFaceExpr best-confidence), tool_call{name,args,result} where name in start_activity(result.audioPlaylist/currentSectionText/sectionNumber/totalSections/interactive)/play_melody(result.filename/playCount/variant original|revised|background/speakText)/end_activity(result.speakText), done{usage} (finalize; callTts full text if not streamed; resume idle), error{message} (gentle fallback; server already auto-retried once; may re-send same turn once). Set concerningMode=true only when risk-assess returned 'concerning' (server forbids start_activity, lowers temp). Set activityContext.sectionIndex / coCreationLastVariant / coCreationNotes from runtime-owned state to advance scripted activities turn-by-turn (server does NOT persist this). net/brain.py's chat_stream already parses this loop correctly. AUDIO PLAYBACK: pipe /api/tts bytes (or decoded /api/tts/visemes base64) to an MP3-capable player (mpg123/ffplay) on the voice channel; play /api/audio/file bytes on the activity-bg + comfort channels. STT/VOICE: key2 mic -> WAV -> /api/transcribe. Voice-Live WS is explicitly OUT of scope for R3.
design.buttonMap: key1 START/STOP — IDLE: press = handleStart (enter INTRO_FIRST_MEETING, speak getFirstMeetingQuestion). Any active session: press = handleEndSession (stop all audio + comfort music, endActivity, face 'calm', -> IDLE). This is the only session on/off. key2 TALK (push-to-talk, the universal input) — in EVERY state that awaits a child reply (all intro steps, both warmup games, weather/pick/decide, free LLM chat, concerning-music-offer): hold = start mic capture (face 'listening'); release/VAD-stop = transcribe -> run_turn(text). Replaces BOTH the text box and the mic button. Sound-detective guesses are open-ended so key2 is REQUIRED there (no button can substitute). The activity-intent bypass works via key2: a spoken '我们直接玩音乐吧' -> classify('activity-intent')=yes -> hand to LLM. key3 NEXT — context-dependent advance: (a) in WARMUP_GAME1 (rhythm-story) = definitive 'I'm done' = transition to WEATHER_CHOICE, hard-skipping the lossy task-completed classifier; (b) in an active ACTIVITY = advance section = run_turn('继续', silent=True) / activityContext.sectionIndex+1 / playlist-next; (c) in GAME_PICK/GAME_DECIDE menus = optionally cycle the offered cards; (d) elsewhere = the silent '继续' auto-advance the code already issues. Always SKIPS the safety gate (silent system advance). key4 REPEAT — (a) default = re-speak last_assistant_line via callTts (re-fetch visemes, replay); (b) in WARMUP_GAME2 = replay the current game2_sound clip (kids want to hear the sound again); (c) after a safety line = replay the last fixed safety line / comfort track. Never changes state. Mute is hardwired unmuted on the robot (mutedRef gate from the studio is dropped). The numeric age answer ('三岁了'/'5') comes from key2 voice -> transcribe -> parseAgeFromText, identical to typed input.

buildOrder.step: S1 — Boot + IDLE face on the LCD driven by a button
buildOrder.deliverable: robot/main.py: face render thread (reuse run_face.py loop) shows 'calm' + daydream on the real LCD; key1 toggles current_expr between 'calm' and 'happy'; brain.health() gate logged at boot. The smallest visible on-robot milestone: a living face that reacts to a physical button.
buildOrder.dependsOn: existing face/renderer.py, face/idle.py, hw/buttons.py, run_face.py, net/brain.py(health)

buildOrder.step: S2 — key2 push-to-talk -> transcribe -> speak (echo loop)
buildOrder.deliverable: Hold key2 = capture 16kHz WAV with RMS VAD; release -> POST /api/transcribe -> speak the returned text back via callTts (/api/tts/visemes) with the mouth lip-syncing to visemes and idle suppressed while speaking. Proves the full mic->brain->speaker->face embodiment seam end to end.
buildOrder.dependsOn: S1; add mic capture (sounddevice/arecord) + tts_visemes() in net/brain.py + an MP3 player with on-ended callback

buildOrder.step: S3 — Safety gate + classify scaffolding (run_turn pipeline)
buildOrder.deliverable: run_turn(text,silent,override): pauseComfortMusic -> /api/risk-assess (FIX endpoint to /api/risk-assess + read risk_level/emotion) -> keyword filter -> safe emotion drives face. high_risk speaks HIGH_RISK_RESPONSE + ends; concerning speaks CONCERNING_RESPONSE + offers round-robin comfort music. A reusable classify(transcript,schema) helper. This de-risks the highest-stakes path first.
buildOrder.dependsOn: S2; safety.distressKeywords/comfortMusicFiles from bundle; 3rd (comfort) audio channel

buildOrder.deliverable: current_step machine: INTRO_FIRST_MEETING -> {INTRO_AGE | INTRO_RETURNING_AGE -> INTRO_RETURNING_ANSWER}; parseAgeFromText (ASCII+Chinese numerals); conversationFlow constants from config; dispatchScriptedOrBypass (activity-intent) wrapping each step; goodbye suppression while step!='none'. Robot now opens a session exactly like TestChat up to the warmup hand-off.

buildOrder.step: S5 — The two warmup games + weather/recommend funnel
buildOrder.deliverable: WARMUP_GAME1 (rhythm-story, key3=done shortcut), WARMUP_GAME2 (sound-detective, sound from bundle audioManifest, key4=replay), then WEATHER_CHOICE -> GAME_PICK -> GAME_DECIDE -> GAME_POOL_EXHAUSTED. Game-decide 'yes' fires the silent start_activity message into the LLM path (stubbed until S6).
buildOrder.dependsOn: S4; /api/audio/file playback on the sfx channel

buildOrder.step: S6 — LLM free-chat + ACTIVITY sub-FSM (the 4 activities)
buildOrder.deliverable: step 'none' consumes /api/chat SSE: speak text events verbatim, setFaceExpr from expression timeline, handle start_activity/play_melody/end_activity tool_calls with runtime-owned activityContext (sectionIndex, coCreationLastVariant/Notes), activity-bg-music channel with playCount/loop/20s-emotion-timer/90s-watchdog, key3 section advance, goodbye detection. Full R3 experience runs on 4 buttons + voice.
buildOrder.dependsOn: S5; activity-bg audio channel with reliable ended callbacks; chat_stream tool_call handling

buildOrder.step: S7 — Hardening: REPEAT everywhere, watchdogs, autostart
buildOrder.deliverable: key4 REPEAT across all states; auto-resend on chat error event; mic re-prompt on NoMatch; 90s activity watchdog; comfort_music_cursor persistence; bundle hot-reload on /api/publish/active change; systemd autostart replacing the Yahboom demo (R5 seam).

risks.risk: net/brain.py calls the WRONG safety endpoint: POST /api/risk and reads a {level} key, but the server route is /api/risk-assess returning {emotion, risk_level}. If shipped as-is the distress gate silently no-ops (404 swallowed -> fails safe to neutral), defeating the single most important safety rule.
risks.mitigation: Fix in S3 before any child testing: rename to /api/risk-assess, parse risk_level + emotion. Add a startup self-test that asserts a known high_risk phrase returns high_risk; keep the local distressKeywords keyword filter as the independent second net so a brain hiccup never fully disables distress detection.

risks.risk: Two emotion->expression paths fight and config.emotionRouting is dead. The risk classifier emotion sets the face immediately, then ~1-3s later the chat expression timeline overrides it; config.emotionRouting (joy/fear/anger labels) is never read and its labels don't even match the classifier's (happy/scared/angry).
risks.mitigation: Port the hardcoded 7-label switch verbatim for v1 (prompt says reproduce exactly), single faceExpr (no scheduled sub-utterance timeline). Document the gap and surface it as an open decision: optionally wire config.emotionRouting as source-of-truth later. Define precedence: latest setter wins (risk emotion first, then stream expression), matching TestChat.

risks.risk: Lip-sync clock: the web port indexes visemes by HTMLAudioElement.currentTime each rAF frame. A Pi MP3 player (mpg123/ffplay) may not expose an accurate playback position, breaking mouth alignment.
risks.mitigation: Prefer a player/lib that reports position (pygame.mixer, or sounddevice with a decoded PCM buffer and a sample counter). Fallback: wall-clock from play-start aligned to visemes[].audioOffsetMs. Validate in S2 with a known utterance. If lip-sync is too costly, degrade to /api/tts (no visemes) + audio-envelope mouth — face still alive, mouth just less precise.

risks.risk: Three concurrent audio channels (voice/sfx, activity-bg with fades+loops, comfort) plus reliable end-of-track callbacks are assumed by the activity timers (co-creation playCount, emotion-mapping 20s windows, 90s watchdog, waitForEnd sequencing). The browser got free mixing from multiple HTMLAudio elements; a naive Pi player blocks or can't mix.
risks.mitigation: Use pygame.mixer (multiple Channels + per-channel volume + set_endevent callbacks) or sounddevice streams. Implement the 90s watchdog and on-error force-advance from day one (S6) so an activity never stalls. Test waitForEnd ordering (safety line, game intro->sound->question) explicitly in S5.

risks.risk: ASR mis-transcription is far higher-variance than typing: a misheard age ('三岁'->'伞岁'), a 'yes' read as a goodbye, or a vague guess can derail the deterministic intro. parseAgeFromText may get null/garbage; goodbye could fire mid-intro.
risks.mitigation: Keep goodbyePromise forced false while step!='none' (port verbatim). Re-prompt on classify 'unclear' instead of guessing. Use AGE_DEFAULT bucket when parseAgeFromText fails. On /api/transcribe NoMatch/silence, speak a gentle re-prompt and re-capture rather than feeding empty text into classify. Confirm the AGE_DEFAULT bucket with the team.

risks.risk: start_activity / co-creation depend on the runtime correctly OWNING activityContext (sectionIndex, coCreationLastVariant, coCreationNotes) — the server does NOT persist it between /api/chat calls. If the Python FSM mis-tracks section index or co-creation stage, the server forces the wrong tool and activities desync.
risks.mitigation: Port TestChat's activityContext bookkeeping exactly into the ACTIVITY sub-FSM; advance sectionIndex only on confirmed section completion (audio-ended or key3); snapshot-test the co-creation variant progression (none->original->revised->background) against the studio in S6. Speak ONLY server text events (never re-TTS tool_call.speakText/currentSectionText, which the server already duplicates as text).

risks.risk: high_risk silently ends the session with no caregiver signal — acceptable on a screen-watched studio, but the robot has no operator watching. A child in crisis could be left with just a spoken hotline and a dark face.
risks.mitigation: Ship the verbatim spoken HIGH_RISK_RESPONSE + gentle->calm face for v1 (distress escalation is cut from v1 per CLAUDE.md), but flag a physical caregiver affordance (distinct LED pattern / chime / nurse-call GPIO / localhost ping) as an explicit open decision for the team, since the physical context differs from the studio's assumptions.

missingBehaviors: BREAK SUGGESTION after N free-form turns is completely absent from the design's FSM. TestChat tracks userTurnCount/breakDue (refs at L1009-1010, incremented only on real, non-silent, non-override LLM turns when step==='none' AND no active activity, L2306-2320). When the count crosses getMaxTurnsBeforeBreak() (config maxTurnsBeforeBreak, default 8, L1071-1072), breakDue is set. In onDone (L2632-2662) it injects a SEPARATE assistant bubble with a pickBreakSuggestion() phrase (config.conversationFlow.breakSuggestionPhrases, fallback hardcoded), deferred 1500ms after the just-finished TTS so they don't overlap, and ONLY if no activity/scripted step started in the meantime (otherwise the structured rest counts as the break and breakDue is silently cleared). A child WILL notice the robot never offering a rest after a long chat. The design lists user_turn_count/break_due in the refs paragraph but NO state, transition, or side-effect ever reads or fires them.

EMOTION-MUSIC-MAPPING DETERMINISTIC CLOSING -> game-pick is missing. When the last emotion section's 20s timer fires (L2547-2593, the onComplete callback to startEmotionMappingTimer), the activity does NOT auto-restart or hand to the model: it ends the activity, pauses activity audio, seeds gamePool=['rhythm','breathing','co-creation'], marks introducedGames={'emotion-mapping'}, speaks the fixed EMOTION_MAPPING_CLOSING line (L126), sets step='game-pick', and routes the child's next answer back into the scripted game-pick machine (so 音乐心情猜猜猜 can never auto-replay). The design's LLM/ACTIVITY state treats end_activity uniformly and never models this special closing that re-enters the scripted funnel.

CONTENT-FILTER 400 mid-LLM-turn is a SECOND crisis-end path the design omits. In onError (L2664-2707), an Azure content-filter 400 (content_filter / ResponsibleAIPolicyViolation / 'filtered') is treated like a distress hit: it flags the bubble, sets face 'anxious'->'calm', and calls handleEndSession() — ending the session even though /api/risk-assess said safe. The design only ends on high_risk/keyword at the SAFETY_GATE; it never handles a content-filter error event from /api/chat as a session-ender (the design's error state just says 'gentle fallback / auto-retry once').

GOODBYE is consumed AFTER the assistant TTS finishes, inside the finally of EVERY scripted step and onDone (L1730, 1777, 1817, 1858, 1888, 1907, 1958, 1985, 2041, 2066, 2130, 2150, 2175, 2196, 2246, 2292, 2538), via `if (await goodbyePromise) handleEndSession()`. goodbyePromise is fired in PARALLEL at L1547-1550 but only when step==='none' (else forced false). The design mentions goodbye suppression during intro and goodbye in the LLM state, but does not capture that goodbye is awaited post-TTS in scripted steps too (so a child who is past intro, at step 'none', saying 'bye' still hears the reply, THEN the session ends).

The 'age' new-friend path stores age and speaks the weather prompt but sets step='none' so the weather answer goes to the LLM (L1741). The design's INTRO_AGE state captures this, but the design's WEATHER_CHOICE/GAME_PICK funnel is therefore only reachable on the RETURNING path (via warmup) — the design does not make explicit that a NEW friend never enters the scripted weather->recommend->game funnel at all, a notable asymmetry a child on a fresh session would experience (free LLM chat right after the weather question, no game cards).

gaps.behavior: key3=NEXT as a 'definitive done' hard-skip in WARMUP_GAME1 (rhythm-story)
gaps.whyItMatters: The design INVENTS this transition ('key3=NEXT press -> WEATHER_CHOICE (treat as definitive done; skips the lossy classifier)') and presents it as a faithful mapping, but it does not exist in TestChat. The real game-1-completion handler (L2253-2294) ALWAYS runs classify('task-completed'); on 'no' it un-appends the turn and dumps the child into free LLM chat (L2267-2275), losing the weather funnel. Shipping the invented NEXT shortcut changes behavior (a child pressing NEXT would skip a classifier path that, in the studio, decides whether to abandon the scripted flow). It may be a good affordance, but the design must flag it as an ADDITION, not a port — the prompt says reproduce the experience exactly.
gaps.whereInCode: apps/studio/src/panels/TestChat.tsx L2253-2294 (real handler has no button path); design GAME_DECIDE/WARMUP_GAME1 transitions

gaps.behavior: concerningMode is NEVER sent to /api/chat in the real flow
gaps.whyItMatters: The design's protocol section explicitly instructs: 'Set concerningMode=true only when risk-assess returned concerning (server forbids start_activity, lowers temp)'. But the real TestChat concerning path (L1498-1540) short-circuits BEFORE any /api/chat call: it speaks the fixed CONCERNING_RESPONSE constant and moves to 'concerning-music-offer'. It never calls the LLM for that turn. The in-code COMMENT at L1382-1388 describing concerningMode is stale (the safety map already flagged this). The server DOES accept concerningMode (chat.ts:73, 443, 511) but TestChat doesn't use it. A Python runtime following the design's protocol literally would call the LLM on concerning turns and skip the fixed comfort reply + music offer — a child in mild distress would get a model-generated reply instead of the deterministic comfort script + soft-music offer. The design's LLM-state transition ('concerning -> CONCERNING_RESPONSE -> CONCERNING_MUSIC_OFFER') is correct, so the design contradicts itself; the protocol line must be removed/corrected.
gaps.whereInCode: TestChat.tsx L1498-1540 (fixed reply, no chat call) vs design.protocol '(3) POST /api/risk-assess ... concerning -> call /api/chat with concerningMode=true' and design.states LLM

gaps.behavior: brain.py classify() omits the context parameter required by sound-match
gaps.whyItMatters: The design correctly catches the /api/risk -> /api/risk-assess + {level}->{risk_level,emotion} bug. But it does NOT flag that net/brain.py classify() (L116-119) posts only {text, schema} with no context field, while sound-detective grading REQUIRES context=sound.label (TestChat L407 isSoundAnswerCorrect -> classifyIntent(text,'sound-match',expectedLabel); server classify.ts:213-216 appends 'Expected: <context>'). Without context the sound-match classifier has no expected answer to compare against and grading degrades. The risk-assess context (last robot line, sliced -300) is also unsupported by brain.py risk(). Both are required client changes the design's risk list misses.
gaps.whereInCode: robot/net/brain.py L116-125; classify.ts L189-216; TestChat.tsx L405-412, L1408-1412

gaps.behavior: Activity auto-advance is timer/audio-ended driven and runs WITHOUT any button press
gaps.whyItMatters: The design's buttonMap leans on key3=NEXT for section advance, but in TestChat scripted activities (body-rhythm/breathing/emotion-mapping) advance AUTONOMOUSLY via silent '继续' on timers (onDone schedules fallbackDelay 8-90s, L2595-2611; emotion-mapping fixed 20s/section, L2550-2554; co-creation on audio 'ended', L803-806). The child never has to press anything to move through a scripted activity. The design mentions the timers in embodimentLoop/risks but its ACTIVITY state machine frames advancement around key3, under-emphasizing that the robot MUST fire silent '继续' on its own clock/end-of-track or activities stall. A child would notice an activity that freezes waiting for a button that the studio never required.
gaps.whereInCode: TestChat.tsx L2541-2623 (onDone auto-advance), L786-818 (audio ended -> 继续), L845-893 (emotion 20s timer); design buttonMap key3 / embodimentLoop

gaps.behavior: emotion-from-risk face override only applies on SAFE turns and is overwritten almost immediately by 'listening'
gaps.whyItMatters: The design says safe-turn risk emotion drives the face via the hardcoded switch, then stream expression overrides. But in TestChat the emotion-derived setFaceExpr (L1431) is set during the safety gate, then sendMessage continues and L2365 sets 'listening', then dispatch sets 'thinking' — so the risk emotion expression is visible only very briefly (or not at all for scripted steps that immediately set 'thinking'). The design's claim that the risk emotion 'drives the face the instant the child speaks' overstates its visible effect for scripted turns; on the robot, faithfully porting means the emotion flash is mostly masked. Minor, but the design presents it as a meaningful livening behavior.
gaps.whereInCode: TestChat.tsx L1420-1432 (emotion switch) then L2365 'listening' / L1622,1676 'thinking'

gaps.behavior: play_melody (co-creation) is QUEUED and released only after the current TTS ends, not played immediately
gaps.whyItMatters: The design says onToolCall play_melody -> play file. But TestChat does NOT play immediately: onToolCall stores it in pendingMelodyRef (L2478-2496) and flushPendingMelody fires only after the assistant's TTS for that turn finishes (callTts .then at L2531-2536, or on audio 'play'/'ended' at L1206-1213) so music doesn't overlap the narration. A naive Python port that plays the melody on the tool_call event would talk over its own co-creation narration. The design's tool_call handling omits this deferral.
gaps.whereInCode: TestChat.tsx L619-628 (pendingMelodyRef/flushPendingMelody), L2478-2496 (queue), L1206-1213 & L2531-2536 (release after TTS)

recommendedAdditions: Add a BREAK_SUGGESTION crosscutting behavior to the FSM: maintain user_turn_count (increment only on real, non-silent, non-override turns while step=='none' and no active activity) and break_due; on the onDone of an LLM turn, if break_due and still no activity/scripted step, speak a pickBreakSuggestion() phrase as a SEPARATE utterance ~1.5s after the main TTS, append it to apiHistory, then clear break_due. Reset both on session start/end. Source maxTurnsBeforeBreak + breakSuggestionPhrases from config.conversationFlow.

Model the EMOTION_MAPPING_CLOSING -> game-pick re-entry explicitly: when the last emotion-mapping section's 20s window completes, deterministically end the activity, seed gamePool=['rhythm','breathing','co-creation'] + introducedGames={'emotion-mapping'}, speak EMOTION_MAPPING_CLOSING, set step='game-pick'. Do NOT let the model wrap up emotion-mapping (it would auto-restart the activity).

Correct the protocol contradiction: REMOVE the instruction to send concerningMode=true on concerning turns. The concerning path must speak the fixed CONCERNING_RESPONSE locally and enter concerning-music-offer WITHOUT calling /api/chat, exactly like TestChat. (Leave concerningMode documented as a server capability that the studio runtime does not exercise.)

Add a content-filter-error session-ender to the LLM/chat error handling: if /api/chat emits an error event that is a content-filter/ResponsibleAIPolicy 400 (not a transient drop), treat it like distress — face anxious->calm, do not speak the raw error, end the session. Only auto-retry on TRANSIENT drops, not on content-filter blocks.

Fix net/brain.py beyond the risk endpoint: (a) classify() must accept and POST an optional context (required for sound-match expected-label and for risk-assess's last-robot-line context); (b) risk() must POST /api/risk-assess with context and return {emotion, risk_level}; (c) add tts_visemes() (only tts() exists today) for LCD lip-sync. Add a boot self-test asserting a known high_risk phrase returns high_risk.

Re-label the key3=NEXT 'definitive done' shortcut in WARMUP_GAME1 as an explicit ADDITION (not a faithful port) and decide with the team whether it should hard-skip the task-completed classifier. Faithful behavior: always classify('task-completed'); on 'no', abandon the scripted weather funnel and re-dispatch the raw text to the LLM.

Make autonomous activity advancement first-class in the ACTIVITY sub-FSM: the runtime must emit silent '继续' on (i) emotion-mapping 20s/section timer, (ii) co-creation audio 'ended'/error/watchdog, (iii) scripted body-rhythm/breathing onDone fallbackDelay (length-scaled when muted, 90s safety net otherwise) — none of which require a button. key3=NEXT is an OPTIONAL manual advance layered on top, not the primary driver.

Defer co-creation play_melody playback until the turn's narration TTS has finished (mirror pendingMelodyRef/flushPendingMelody), so background/original/revised tracks never overlap the spoken line; honor playCount (background=2 with fade on final pass).

finalDesign.rationale: Verified against the real code, the split is unambiguous. The orchestration that must move to the robot is ~95% UI-state choreography meaningless on the server; the heavy lifting is already pure server primitives the Pi calls over HTTP (verified: /api/chat SSE, /api/tts, /api/tts/visemes, /api/classify, /api/risk-assess, /api/transcribe, /api/audio/file all live on the Node brain). (1) The scripted intro + 2 warmup games + safety gate + recommended-game funnel are a deterministic client FSM keyed on one string (scriptedSessionStepRef) plus a handful of refs (childAgeRef, game2SoundRef L1024, gamePoolRef L1032, introducedGamesRef L1033, currentGameRef L1034, comfortMusicCursor, userTurnCountRef L1009, breakDueRef L1010). Every transition is classify(transcript)->label->speak a fixed conversationFlow constant->set next step; none of it touches Azure directly — it only sequences /api/classify, /api/risk-assess, /api/tts(/visemes), /api/audio/file. net/brain.py already implements the HTTP client. (2) The genuinely server-side intelligence — LLM chat with tool_calls (start_activity/play_melody/end_activity), clause-sentiment expression timeline, the risk classifier with content-filter fail-closed, the 10 classify schemas, the TTS+SSML sanitizer, STT — is ALREADY in apps/server and verified on the Pi at 127.0.0.1:8787. The 4 activities run server-side via the tool_call protocol; the runtime is a sequencer of text/expression/tool_call/done events. (3) extract-fsm-to-server-node is wrong because transitions are driven by EMBODIMENT events the server cannot see: button edges (key1/2/3/4 in hw/buttons.py poll()/is_held()), audio 'ended' callbacks (the waitForEnd sequencing L1490, co-creation audio-ended advance L803-806, playCount loops, emotion-mapping 20s timers L2554, 90s watchdog L2603), viseme playback-clock position, mic VAD endpointing. A server FSM would need a bidirectional event channel back for every press and track-end — a worse Voice-Live. (4) The runtime MUST own activityContext (sectionIndex L2376/L2460-2461, coCreationLastVariant L2399/L2488-2492, coCreationNotes L2493-2496) because the server does NOT persist it between /api/chat calls (verified L2396-2418 rebuilds it every turn) — this reinforces python-reimplement-fsm. A hybrid is unnecessary; no third slice wants the server. The few INVENTED affordances the critic flagged (key3 hard-skip in rhythm-story; key4 REPEAT; concerningMode on /api/chat) are corrected to faithful behavior or explicitly marked as additions below.
finalDesign.fsmOverview: One top-level session FSM in robot/session/fsm.py, a faithful port of TestChat's scriptedSessionStep machine plus the LLM handoff (step=='none') and safety pre-emption. States: BOOT -> IDLE -> (key1) INTRO_FIRST_MEETING -> {INTRO_AGE | INTRO_RETURNING_AGE -> INTRO_RETURNING_ANSWER} -> WARMUP_GAME1|WARMUP_GAME2 -> WEATHER_CHOICE -> GAME_PICK -> GAME_DECIDE -> LLM (step 'none', free chat + ACTIVITY sub-FSM) -> GOODBYE -> IDLE. ASYMMETRY (verified L1741): a NEW friend's 'age' step sets step='none', so the new-friend weather answer goes to the LLM and a new friend NEVER enters the scripted weather->recommend->game funnel; only the RETURNING path reaches WEATHER_CHOICE (via a warmup game). The robot reproduces this asymmetry exactly. CROSSCUTTING behaviors wrap the step machine, all verified in source: (a) SAFETY_GATE runs BEFORE the step machine on every non-silent/non-override turn (L1399-1453): high_risk OR keyword-hit -> BLOCKED_CRISIS -> end; concerning -> CONCERNING_RESPONSE (a FIXED client reply, NOT a /api/chat call) -> CONCERNING_MUSIC_OFFER, session stays alive. (b) BREAK_SUGGESTION (L2306-2320, L2632-2662): user_turn_count increments only on real non-silent/non-override turns while step=='none' AND no active activity; at maxTurnsBeforeBreak (default 8) break_due is set; in the LLM onDone, if break_due and STILL no activity/scripted step, speak a pickBreakSuggestion() phrase as a SEPARATE utterance ~1.5s after the main TTS, append to apiHistory, clear break_due (if a structured rest started in the meantime, silently clear break_due instead). (c) CONTENT_FILTER_END (L2664-2707): a content-filter/ResponsibleAIPolicy error event from /api/chat is a SECOND crisis-end path — face anxious->calm, do NOT speak the raw error, end the session; only TRANSIENT drops auto-retry. (d) GOODBYE is awaited post-TTS in EVERY scripted step's finally and in onDone via `if (await goodbyePromise) handleEndSession()` (L2538), but goodbyePromise is forced false while step!='none' (L1547-1550) so an ASR'd '是/嗯/对' can't end the session mid-intro; only at step=='none' can goodbye fire. The ACTIVITY sub-FSM (driven by /api/chat tool_call+text, runtime owns activityContext) runs while step=='none' after a start_activity fires; it auto-advances on its OWN clock/track-end (NOT requiring a button) and has a deterministic emotion-mapping closing that RE-ENTERS the scripted game-pick funnel. The crucial inversion vs the web app: TestChat is React-event-driven; the robot is a single main loop that renders the face every frame, polls buttons, and on a finalized utterance string runs run_turn(text) — the same pipeline: pauseComfortMusic -> risk-assess+keyword -> dispatch (BLOCKED/CONCERNING/scripted-step-via-dispatchScriptedOrBypass/LLM). The intro never calls /api/chat; only /api/classify, /api/risk-assess, /api/tts(/visemes), /api/audio/file. The LLM only enters at step 'none'.

states.name: BOOT
states.purpose: Bring the runtime up and confirm the Node brain is reachable before any child interaction.
transitions: -> IDLE once /api/health returns ok and config+bundle loaded
states.sideEffects: Loop on brain.health() until ok (gate the FSM). Load active StudioBundle via bundle.load_active (audioManifest, config.face.expressionLibrary colors, conversationFlow incl. maxTurnsBeforeBreak/breakSuggestionPhrases, games, safety incl. distressKeywords/comfortMusicFiles). Cache get_config('default') into convFlow/games/safety refs. Run a startup self-test: brain.risk('我不想活了') MUST return risk_level=='high_risk' (asserts the FIXED /api/risk-assess endpoint works); log loudly if not. Start the face render thread at 'calm' with idle daydream. No audio.

states.name: IDLE
states.purpose: Session-idle. Face daydreams; wait for key1=START.
states.sideEffects: setFaceExpr('calm'); idle loop (blink/breath/drift) active. All audio channels silent. comfort_music_cursor persists. On entry from a prior session (handleEndSession reset, L1342-1362): current_step='none', child_age=default, clear transcript/apiHistory/game refs/pool, user_turn_count=0, break_due=false, sessionActive=false.

states.name: INTRO_FIRST_MEETING
states.purpose: Port of handleStartChatting + 'first-meeting'. Ask if first meeting; branch new vs returning.
states.sideEffects: On entry (key1): refresh config, clear state, user_turn_count=0, break_due=false, sessionActive=true. Speak getFirstMeetingQuestion() via callTts; face gentle->calm 500ms after. Each child reply: face 'thinking' while classifying. yes -> speak getStartChattingIntro() (which itself asks age). no -> speak OLD_FRIEND_RECOGNITION + getAgePrompt(). goodbyePromise forced false while step!='none'.

states.name: INTRO_AGE
states.purpose: New-friend age capture; deliver age-gated weather/mood prompt, then hand to LLM (NOT the scripted funnel).
transitions: age parsed (parseAgeFromText) -> LLM (step 'none'; the weather answer is LLM-handled)
states.sideEffects: parseAgeFromText(transcript) handles ASCII+Chinese numerals; if in [1,120] set child_age (else AGE_DEFAULT). Speak weatherPromptForAge(getWeatherPrompt(), age) — drops snow option for age<=7. step->'none' (verified L1741: NEW friend never enters WEATHER_CHOICE; the weather answer goes to the model). face gentle->calm.

states.name: INTRO_RETURNING_AGE
states.purpose: Returning-friend age capture, then daily-story intro.
states.sideEffects: Parse+store age (AGE_DEFAULT on fail). Speak OLD_FRIEND_AGE_TO_STORY + a random pickReturningIntroFromConfig() daily story (ends '你今天过得怎么样呢?'). step->'returning-intro-answer'. face gentle->calm.

states.name: INTRO_RETURNING_ANSWER
states.purpose: Mood-mirror reply to the daily story, then launch ONE coin-flip warmup game.
states.sideEffects: moodReply via classify('mood') -> one of 4 fixed mirror lines. Game2 branch: speak moodReply+getGame2Intro() (waitForEnd=True) -> playSoundEffect(sound.src from /api/audio/file, await ended) -> speak sound.question; set game2_sound; step->'game-2-answer'. Game1 branch: speak moodReply+getGame1Prefix()+random rhythm story; step->'game-1-completion'. face thinking->gentle.

states.name: WARMUP_GAME1 (game-1-completion)
states.purpose: Rhythm-story: child claps then says done; detect completion via classifier.
transitions: classify('task-completed')=='no' -> LLM (abandon scripted funnel, re-send raw text — verified L2267-2275)
[ADDITION] key3=NEXT -> treat as definitive done -> WEATHER_CHOICE (see buttonMap; NOT in TestChat, flagged for team sign-off)
states.sideEffects: face 'thinking' while classifying. yes -> speak random pickGame1Completion() + weatherPromptForAge(); step->'weather-game-choice'; face gentle->calm. no -> un-append turn, step->'none', re-send raw text to LLM. No prerecorded audio (pure TTS narration). FAITHFUL default is ALWAYS classify('task-completed'); the key3 hard-skip is an explicitly-flagged ADDITION, not a port.

states.name: WARMUP_GAME2 (game-2-answer)
states.purpose: Sound-detective: grade the child's guess leniently, respond, then enter weather funnel.
transitions: key4=REPEAT press -> replay game2_sound clip, stay in state
states.sideEffects: isSoundAnswerCorrect = classify('sound-match', text, context=sound.label e.g. '鸡 Chicken') -> correctResponse/wrongResponse. REQUIRES brain.classify to send context (server appends 'Expected: <label>', classify.ts L213-216). fixedReply = baseReply + weatherPromptForAge(). game2_sound=None after answering. face thinking->gentle->calm. Sound resolved from bundle audioManifest by exact audioFilename.

states.name: WEATHER_CHOICE (weather-game-choice) — RETURNING path only
states.purpose: Child names a weather standing for mood; seed the recommended-game pool. Reached only via a returning-friend warmup (verified asymmetry).
states.sideEffects: face 'thinking'. Speak SUNNY_RECOMMENDATION or quietRecommendationLocal(mirror). Reset introduced_games=set(), current_game=None. Pool entries are RECOMMENDED_GAMES ids = activity ALIASES (rhythm/co-creation/breathing/emotion-mapping), not real GameConfigs.

states.name: GAME_PICK (game-pick)
states.purpose: Child names one of the 4 recommended games; show its card, ask whether to start. Also the RE-ENTRY target after the emotion-mapping deterministic closing. NOT wrapped in activity-intent bypass (bare game names would falsely trip it).
transitions: unclear -> GAME_PICK (re-ask via secondary classify('yesno'): vague vs unmatched line)
states.sideEffects: face 'thinking'. On match: remove from pool, introduced_games.add, current_game=id, speak RECOMMENDED_GAMES[id].card + GAME_DECIDE_PROMPT. (Entered here directly by the EMOTION_MAPPING_CLOSING handler with pool pre-seeded ['rhythm','breathing','co-creation'] and introduced_games={'emotion-mapping'}.)

states.name: GAME_DECIDE (game-decide)
states.purpose: Start the chosen activity, switch to a different card, or browse the next. NOT bypass-wrapped.
transitions: classify('yesno')=='yes' -> LLM+START_ACTIVITY (silent message forces start_activity tool)
named different valid game (yesno!='yes') -> GAME_DECIDE (re-route to that card)
states.sideEffects: Run classify('yesno') and classify('game-name') in PARALLEL; yes/no wins ties. yes -> speak GAME_START_LINE, face 'excited', step->'none', then run_turn('开始'+displayName+'吧', silent=True) so the LLM calls start_activity. Hand-off to ACTIVITY sub-FSM.

states.name: GAME_POOL_EXHAUSTED (game-pool-exhausted)
states.purpose: All recommended games declined; drop scaffolding to the LLM.
states.sideEffects: step->'none'; re-dispatch the child's text to /api/chat (non-silent). All subsequent turns are free-form LLM.

states.name: LLM (step 'none' free chat + ACTIVITY sub-FSM)
states.purpose: Free-form conversation and the 4 activities, driven by /api/chat SSE (text/expression/tool_call/done/error).
transitions: tool_call start_activity -> enter ACTIVITY (own sectionIndex from L2460-2461)
tool_call end_activity -> clear activity, back to free chat
emotion-mapping last-section 20s timer fires -> EMOTION_MAPPING_CLOSING (NOT model wrap-up)
classify('goodbye')=='yes' awaited post-TTS (only when step=='none') -> GOODBYE
key3=NEXT -> manual section advance: run_turn('继续', silent=True)
SAFETY_GATE still pre-empts every real turn
states.sideEffects: face 'thinking' on dispatch; speak ONLY server text events verbatim (server authors scripted narration and SUPPRESSES the model's pre-tool lead-in — do NOT re-TTS tool_call.speakText/currentSectionText). expression timeline -> setFaceExpr(best-confidence) (single faceExpr, not scheduled). onDone -> callTts(full text) then schedule auto-advance and break-suggestion. AUTONOMOUS advance (no button needed): emotion-mapping fixed 20s/section timer (L2554); co-creation on activity-audio 'ended' -> silent '继续' (L803-806); scripted body-rhythm/breathing onDone fallbackDelay (length-scaled when muted, else 90s watchdog L2603). play_melody is QUEUED in pending_melody and FLUSHED only after the turn's TTS ends (L2481-2496, L2531-2536) so music never overlaps narration; playCount honored (background=2 then fade+auto-advance). Runtime OWNS activityContext {sectionIndex, coCreationLastVariant, coCreationNotes}; advance sectionIndex only on confirmed section completion.

states.name: SAFETY_GATE (crosscutting, every real turn)
states.purpose: Two-layer distress screen run BEFORE the step machine on every non-silent, non-override utterance.
